title: Apply minimum release age delay to manual extension updates
document:
Apply minimum release age delay to manual extension updates

This is further to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/316867 and https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/321136. To avoid supply chain attacks, support was added for an "auto update delay" for auto-updated extensions.

But as noted in those issues, there is also a scenario in which updates are performed manually. Many of us don't blindly auto-update.

In such a case, 1) embargoed updates should be hidden until the delay is reached, or 2) if they are shown then the UI should caution the user that they are still embargoed.

I've added this dedicated feature request so it isn't forgotten.

Thank you!

title: Integrated terminal enters inconsistent state after restart: "Restart Terminal" warnings, xterm.js Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'dimensions'), terminal shortcuts intermittently stop working
document:
Integrated terminal enters inconsistent state after restart: "Restart Terminal" warnings, xterm.js Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'dimensions'), terminal shortcuts intermittently stop working

### VS Code Version

### OS

Windows 11 Home Single Language
Version 25H2
Build 26200.8655

### Extensions

Only Microsoft Python extensions:

- ms-python.python
- ms-python.debugpy
- ms-python.vscode-pylance
- ms-python.vscode-python-envs

Issue also occurred with GitHub Copilot enabled previously.

---

## Reproduction

1. Open a Python project with a local venv.
2. Open the integrated terminal.
3. Close VS Code.
4. Reopen VS Code.
5. After reopening, the integrated terminal frequently enters a bad state.

---

## Symptoms

- The terminal status shows a yellow warning icon.
- VS Code repeatedly asks to restart the terminal because extensions want to modify the terminal environment.
- The extension mentioned changes between sessions (Python Debugger, Copilot, etc.).
- Python virtual environment activation becomes inconsistent.
- Terminal keyboard shortcuts intermittently stop working (Ctrl+C, Ctrl+V, Ctrl+A, Ctrl+S).
- Sometimes Ctrl combinations are passed directly to the shell (e.g. `^A`) instead of being handled by VS Code.
- Creating a new terminal sometimes fixes the issue temporarily.

---

## What I already tested

- Clean VS Code profile (`--user-data-dir`)
- Reinstalled VS Code
- Deleted User/globalStorage/workspaceStorage
- Disabled shell integration
- Disabled persistent terminal sessions
- Downgraded VS Code to 1.126.0
- Downgraded Python extensions by several months

Downgrading Python extensions removes the persistent "Restart Terminal" warning, but the terminal keyboard issues still occur.

---

## Developer Console

The following error consistently appears:

Later another error appears:

---

## Additional observations

Keyboard shortcut troubleshooting shows that VS Code successfully resolves shortcuts such as Ctrl+C and invokes the expected commands, yet the integrated terminal still behaves incorrectly afterwards.

This makes it look like the terminal frontend (xterm.js) enters an invalid state after window/session restoration.

title: Custom Endpoint sends invalid Anthropic request (temperature and top_p)
document:
Custom Endpoint sends invalid Anthropic request (temperature and top_p)

Problem

When using an Anthropic model through a Custom Endpoint, VS Code Chat fails with the following error:

Sorry, your request failed. Please try again.

Reason:

400 Bad Request

AnthropicException:
temperature and top_p cannot both be specified for this model

Model

anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6

Configuration

- vendor: customendpoint
- apiType: chat-completions
- Endpoint: https://api.hrz.uni-giessen.de

1. Configure a Custom Endpoint model in chatLanguageModels.json.
2. Select anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 in VS Code Chat.
3. Send any message.
4. The request fails with the error above.

Observed Behavior

The same endpoint, API key and model work correctly in:

- Playground
- OpenAI Python SDK
- Continue extension

The failure occurs only in VS Code Chat.

Additional Information

The endpoint and model are functioning correctly outside VS Code. The issue appears to be specific to the VS Code Custom Endpoint implementation when using Anthropic models through an OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

Could you please verify whether VS Code Custom Endpoint requests automatically include both temperature and top_p when sending requests to Anthropic models?

title: Remote SSH:  oom Issue in versions newer than 1.120.0 when connected via ssh
document:
Remote SSH:  oom Issue in versions newer than 1.120.0 when connected via ssh

- Working Version: 1.120.0
- Issue Observed: All versions greater than 1.120.0 example 1.127.0
- Remote-SSH Extension: Remote - SSH: Editing Configuration Files; Microsoft; 0.87.0

Local Machine: Windows 11; 64-bit

Remote Machine:
- Ubuntu; VERSION=20.04.6
- 16 Core
- Remote connection via SSH

1. Launch Visual Studio Code version 1.127.0 (greater than 1.120.0).
2. Connect to a remote Linux machine using the Remote-SSH extension.
3. Open the same workspace/repository that works correctly with VS Code 1.120.0.
4. Allow the workspace and extensions to initialize.
5. Observe increasing CPU and memory consumption on the remote
host by VS Code related processes.
6. Monitor the remote machine using:
 top
 ps -ef
7. One or more processes named "MainThread" begin consuming excessive
CPU resources (up to ~100% CPU).
8. VS Code becomes sluggish/unresponsive and eventually encounters
memory-related instability/OOM symptoms.
9. Repeat the same steps using VS Code 1.120.0.
10. Observe that the issue does not occur and the remote session remains stable.

Expected Result:
- Remote-SSH connection should remain stable.
- VS Code should operate normally without excessive CPU or memory consumption.
- Behavior should be consistent with VS Code 1.120.0.

Actual Result:
- (reason: 'oom', code:'-536870904')Issue occurs only in VS Code versions newer than 1.120.0.
- High CPU utilization observed on the remote machine.
- Processes displayed as "MainThread" consume significant CPU resources (up to ~100%).
- Remote development experience becomes progressively slower.
- Memory consumption increases over time, eventually resulting in
OOM-related behavior or VS Code instability.
- Issue is reproducible and does not occur with VS Code 1.120.0.

Troubleshooting Performed:
- Deleted ~/.vscode-server and allowed server reinstallation.
- Cleared VS Code caches.
- Modified settings.json using various recommended workarounds.
- Reproduced using the same remote host and workspace.
- Verified sufficient memory availability on the remote machine.
- Verified issue is specific to Remote-SSH usage.
- Verified issue is not reproducible in VS Code 1.120.0.

Additional Information:
top output on remote machine during issue:
Mem: 40960 MiB total
Free: 21806 MiB
Available: 31099 MiB
The remote system is not under memory pressure when the issue starts.

Observed process:
PID 82202
COMMAND: MainThread
CPU: ~99.7%
RES: ~832 MB

Another MainThread process:

PID 82190
CPU: ~3.3%
RES: ~690 MB
This indicates the problem is not caused by insufficient RAM on
the remote host but appears to be a regression introduced after
VS Code 1.120.0 during Remote-SSH operation.

Regression:
VS Code 1.120.0 → No issue
VS Code > 1.120.0 → High CPU / memory growth / eventual OOM behavior

title: Should Browser become visible when Agent opens it?
document:
Should Browser become visible when Agent opens it?

Sometimes the agent needs to use the browser to test its work or fix bugs. If that session is active, the browser automatically opens in the workbench. This can be distracting, especially if the user doesn't need to monitor the agent's live browsing.

**Proposed Solution:**
Instead of opening the browser automatically, we could keep it hidden by default and display a prominent status indicator showing that the agent is currently using the browser, alongside a button to open the live view.

Additionally, we could add a toggle or parameter to the browser tool. If a user explicitly requests it (e.g., "Show me the browser"), the agent would then have permission to force-open the browser view in the workbench.

title: Local stdio MCP server: initial `availableDeferredTools` list omits many registered tools with no signal that they exist
document:
Local stdio MCP server: initial `availableDeferredTools` list omits many registered tools with no signal that they exist

## Environment
- VS Code 1.122.1
- GitHub Copilot Chat 0.50.1
- OS: Windows
- MCP server: local stdio server implemented with `mcp.server.fastmcp.FastMCP`
- Server registered in workspace `.vscode/mcp.json` as a `stdio` server (single Python entrypoint script)

## Summary
When a chat/agent session starts, the `availableDeferredTools` list provided in the initial context for a connected local stdio MCP server contains only a subset of the tools the server actually registers. Tools outside that subset are not mentioned at all — there is no "N more tools available" indicator. They can only be discovered by proactively calling `tool_search` with a matching natural-language query, but nothing in the initial context signals that such hidden capabilities exist. If the agent does not think to search for a specific capability, the tool is effectively invisible for that turn, even though it is fully registered, fully functional, and immediately usable by other MCP clients connected to the same server (verified with Claude Code against the identical server process).

## Expected behavior
Either:
(a) the initial tool list reliably reflects the full registered tool surface of a small/local stdio MCP server (as Claude Code does for the same server), or
(b) the session clearly signals that additional deferred tools exist for a given server (e.g. "13/32 tools from server X shown, N more discoverable via tool_search"), so the agent knows to search instead of assuming a capability doesn't exist.

## Actual behavior
- The MCP server registers 32 tools total, confirmed via direct introspection (`FastMCP._tool_manager._tools.keys()`), all declared identically via `@mcp.tool()`.
- The initial `availableDeferredTools` block for the session listed only 13 of those 32 tool names for this server.
- Tools completely absent from the initial list included (server-side names): `get_folders`, `get_projects`, `get_statuses`, `create_folder`, `ensure_story_folder`, `get_test_cases`, `get_test_case`, `update_test_case`, `find_test_case_by_name`, `upsert_test_case_by_name`, `validate_test_case_collection`, `preview_test_case_upload_plan`, `bulk_upsert_test_cases`, `get_test_cycles`, `get_confluence_spec`, `search_confluence_page`, `bootstrap_story_memory`, `analyze_story_memory_state`, `create_test_case_issue_link`.
- Calling `tool_search` with a targeted query (e.g. "Zephyr folder listing / get_folders tool") did surface `mcp_advanced-qa-a_get_folders`, and invoking it afterward succeeded and returned correct data.
- This confirms the tool is correctly registered and fully functional; the only defect is discoverability at conversation start.

## Impact
Because the agent had no signal that `get_folders` (and similar tools) existed, it initially resorted to heuristic/manual logic (inferring a Zephyr folder id from historical data) instead of calling the correct, available tool. This produced extra unnecessary tool calls, extra reasoning turns, and materially higher cost for a task that a single correct tool call would have resolved directly.

## Steps to reproduce
1. Implement a local stdio MCP server exposing more than ~15-20 tools (e.g. via FastMCP with `@mcp.tool()` per function).
2. Register it in workspace `.vscode/mcp.json` (type `stdio`, command + args pointing at the server script).
3. Start a new Copilot Chat agent session in that workspace.
4. Inspect the `availableDeferredTools` list given in the initial context for that server's tools.
5. Independently enumerate the server's full registered tool set (e.g. small introspection script printing `FastMCP._tool_manager._tools.keys()`).
6. Diff the two lists — observe that several legitimately registered tools are missing from the initial list with zero indication that they exist.
7. Ask the agent to perform a task that specifically needs one of the missing tools (e.g. "list Zephyr folders") without naming the tool. Observe the agent either fails to find it or falls back to a workaround.
8. Have the agent call `tool_search` with a query describing the missing capability — observe it is found and works correctly.
9. Optionally compare with Claude Code connected to the same MCP server: the same tools are available without requiring an equivalent discovery step.

## Ask
- Document (or fix) the rule that decides which MCP tools from a connected server are shown upfront vs deferred behind `tool_search`.
- For small/local stdio servers with a modest tool count, consider surfacing the full tool list upfront, or explicitly signal how many additional tools exist per server so the agent can decide to search.
- Consider aligning local stdio MCP tool-exposure behavior with how Claude Code surfaces tools from the same server.

title: Agents Window Browser: Help user setup browser open action
document:
Agents Window Browser: Help user setup browser open action

### **Goal**

Automatically capture and persist the active browser link within a workspace session, allowing users to relaunch the website in a future session without manual configuration.

### **Core Workflows**

* **Relaunching the Browser:** If no explicit task is configured, clicking the **Play** (or a dedicated **Browser**) action should automatically open the recorded URL.
* **Automatic Server Setup:** If the website requires a local server to run, we should automatically generate a task so the user can spin up the server directly from the Play button.

title: Support installing extensions from git URLs
document:
Support installing extensions from git URLs

## Feature Request

### Description
Allow VS Code to install extensions directly from git URLs, similar to how modern package managers (npm, pip, cargo, etc.) support installing dependencies from git repositories.

### Current Behavior
Currently, extensions can only be installed from:
- The VS Code Marketplace
- Local VSIX files
- The command line using a marketplace extension ID

### Desired Behavior
Users should be able to install extensions directly from git repositories using a git URL, similar to:

Or configured in a `.vscode/extensions.json` manifest:

### Use Cases
- Installing extensions from forks or development branches
- Installing private extensions from private repositories
- Installing unreleased or experimental extensions
- Managing corporate/team-specific extensions outside the marketplace
- Enabling reproducible development environments with specific extension versions

### Benefits
- **Flexibility**: Developers can use cutting-edge or customized extensions
- **Reproducibility**: Teams can pin exact git commits or tags
- **Privacy**: Private extensions can be versioned and distributed via git
- **Development**: Easier local testing and contribution to extension development

### Examples
This pattern is already well-established in popular package managers:
- **npm**: `"package": "git+https://github.com/user/repo.git#commit/branch/tag"`
- **pip**: `package @ git+https://github.com/user/repo.git@tag`
- **Cargo**: `package = { git = "https://github.com/user/repo.git", tag = "v0.1.0" }`
- **Go**: `require github.com/user/repo v0.0.0-20240101120000-abc123def456`

### Implementation Considerations
- Support standard git URLs (https://, ssh://, git+https://)
- Support branch, tag, and commit references
- Handle authentication for private repositories
 - This could be handled automatically by ssl?
- Validate extension integrity
- Cache downloaded extensions appropriately
- Update mechanism for git-sourced extensions along with deep dependencies with git
 - Semver tag wildcard?

title: Copilot Chat: Draft in new chat is lost after switching conversations
document:
Copilot Chat: Draft in new chat is lost after switching conversations

### Problem summary
In a **new Copilot Chat conversation**, if I type a draft in the chat textarea (without sending), switch to another existing conversation, and then return to the new conversation, the typed draft is lost.

### Contrast with current behavior in ongoing sessions
In an **ongoing/existing conversation**, switching away and returning preserves the unsent draft in the textarea.

### Expected behavior
A draft typed in a **new conversation** should persist when switching to another conversation and coming back, matching the behavior of ongoing conversations.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Open Copilot Chat in VS Code.
2. Start a **new chat**.
3. Type text in the input textarea, but do **not** send it.
4. Switch to a different, previously existing conversation.
5. Return to the new chat started in step 2.

### Current behavior
The draft typed in step 3 is cleared or lost.

### Expected result
The draft should still be present after returning to the new chat.

### Environment
- OS: Windows
- Product: Visual Studio Code
- Area: GitHub Copilot Chat

title: Feature Request: Compare two AI chat / agent sessions and show the agent flow differences
document:
Feature Request: Compare two AI chat / agent sessions and show the agent flow differences

### Summary

I would like VS Code / GitHub Copilot Chat to support comparing two AI chat or agent sessions from the sessions list.

In my workflow, I often run the same AI workflow, skill, or agent task in different chat sessions. Even when the input and workspace are similar, the behavior can be very different between sessions. The agent may choose different files, run different commands, use different tools, follow a different reasoning path, or produce different code changes.

It would be very useful to select two sessions in the chat sessions list and compare what flow the AI agent took in each session.

### Problem

Currently, the chat sessions list lets users open and manage previous sessions, but it is difficult to understand why two sessions behaved differently.

For example:

1. I start session A and run a specific AI workflow / skill.
2. I start session B and run the same workflow / skill again.
3. The final result is different.
4. I want to understand what changed:

 * Which prompts or context were different?
 * Which files were inspected?
 * Which tools or commands were run?
 * Which edits were proposed or applied?
 * Which checkpoints or intermediate steps were different?
 * Where did the agent’s flow diverge?

At the moment, this requires manually opening both sessions and reading through them side by side, which is hard for long agentic workflows.

### Proposed feature

Add an option in the chat sessions / Agents window UI to compare two sessions.

Possible UX:

1. In the sessions list, allow multi-select of two chat or agent sessions.
2. Add a context menu action: **Compare Sessions**.
3. Open a comparison view showing the two sessions side by side.
4. Highlight differences in the agent flow.

The comparison could include:

* User prompts
* Model responses
* Tools used
* Terminal commands run
* Files read or modified
* Checkpoints created
* Agent mode / model / skill / custom instruction differences
* Workspace context differences
* Final code changes
* Errors or failed commands
* A summarized “where the flows diverged” section

### Example use case

I have a reusable AI workflow skill. I run it in two different sessions against the same repository. In one session, the agent correctly identifies the relevant files and produces the expected change. In another session, it takes a completely different path and produces a different result.

I want to compare both sessions and understand:

* Did the agent receive different context?
* Did it inspect different files?
* Did it choose a different plan?
* Did it run different commands?
* Did it skip a step from the workflow?
* Did the behavior change because of model/tool/context differences?

### Why this would be useful
This would help users debug agentic workflows and improve repeatability. It would also make AI-assisted development more transparent by showing how a result was produced, not just the final answer.

This is especially important when users are building or relying on repeatable AI workflows, custom instructions, skills, or agent behaviors across multiple sessions.

### Possible implementation ideas

The comparison view could provide:

* Side-by-side timeline of both sessions
* Diff view for prompts and responses
* Tool-call timeline comparison
* File access / file edit comparison
* Command execution comparison
* Checkpoint comparison
* Summary generated from structured session metadata
* Exportable comparison report

### Expected behavior

A user should be able to select two chat / agent sessions and ask VS Code to compare them. VS Code should show both the detailed differences and a high-level summary of how the agent flows differed.

### Actual behavior

Currently, users need to manually inspect each session separately and infer the differences themselves.

### Related area

This is related to chat session history, agent sessions, and exporting or inspecting AI session artifacts.

title: Agent Host: bring back granular "sticky" terminal auto-approve controls (allow for session / always allow exact command line)
document:
Agent Host: bring back granular "sticky" terminal auto-approve controls (allow for session / always allow exact command line)

Building on the recent work to have `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove` and `chat.tools.terminal.enableAutoApprove` respected for Agent Host Copilot (#322633, #322774), I'd like to bring back the more granular, "sticky" approval controls we used to have so users aren't re-prompted for commands they've already OK'd.

Issue:

Today the AH terminal auto-approver (`src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/commandAutoApprover.ts`) only compiles the forwarded `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove` rules and checks each command against them. Unlike the workbench auto-approver (`.../chatAgentTools/browser/tools/commandLineAnalyzer/autoApprove/commandLineAutoApprover.ts`, which has `_getSessionRules` and shows "Auto approved for this session"), the AH side has **no concept of session-scoped approvals**. So once I approve something, there's no way to make it stick for the rest of the session, and I keep getting re-prompted.

This shows up a lot because terminal auto-approve is deliberately picky about sub-commands: a compound command only auto-approves if *every* parsed segment matches a rule, and things like nested `$(...)` substitutions, added write redirects, or combos the tree-sitter parser can't decompose will still prompt.

Potential thoughts/plan forward:

- **Allow `` for this session**
- **Always allow exact command line ``** (persists to the `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove` setting)
- **Allow all commands in this session** — would want to align with the permissions / risk-assessment folks on how this interacts with the session permission model before wiring it up

Pointers for agent:

- AH: `src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/commandAutoApprover.ts`, and `sessionPermissions.ts` (`getAutoApproval`, `permissionKind === 'shell'` path)
- Workbench reference for session-scoped rules: `_getSessionRules` in `.../chatAgentTools/browser/tools/commandLineAnalyzer/autoApprove/commandLineAutoApprover.ts`

title: Test: Copilot OTel Enterprise Policy via MDM
document:
Test: Copilot OTel Enterprise Policy via MDM

Refs: #323171

- [ ] macOS @anthonykim1
- [x] windows @hediet

Complexity: 4

Authors: @zhichli

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23324134%0A%0A&assignees=zhichli)

---

_Companion TPI (server API delivery): #324133_
_Workstream ref_: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog/issues/7940

VS Code now supports enterprise **OpenTelemetry (OTel) policy** via the shared managed-settings solution used by the other Copilot surfaces.

This supplements (and for copilot settings, will eventually supplant) the device configuration specified in our [documentation](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/enterprise/policies).

The supported keys for now (matching a shared spec with the copilot surfaces):

| Managed-setting key | Type | Example | Policy |
|---|---|---|---|
| `telemetry.enabled` | boolean | `true` | `CopilotOtelEnabled` |
| `telemetry.endpoint` | string (uri) | `http://localhost:4318` | `CopilotOtelEndpoint` |
| `telemetry.protocol` | string | `http/json` | `CopilotOtelProtocol`, `CopilotOtelOtlpProtocol` |
| `telemetry.captureContent` | boolean | `true` | `CopilotOtelCaptureContent` |
| `telemetry.lockCaptureContent` | boolean | `true` | `CopilotOtelCaptureContent` |
| `telemetry.serviceName` | string | `copilot-policy-smoke-test` | `CopilotOtelServiceName` |
| `telemetry.resourceAttributes` | JSON string | `{"deployment.environment":"local","service.namespace":"vscode-testing-policy","policy.source":"managed-settings"}` | `CopilotOtelResourceAttributes` |
| `telemetry.headers` | JSON string | `{"x-copilot-policy-test":"managed-settings","x-service-name":"copilot-policy-smoke-test"}` | `CopilotOtelHeaders` |

# Task

## macOS

#### copilot.mobileconfig

2. Open 'Local Group Policy Editor' and configure settings with the values in the example table above.

#### GitHubCopilot.admx

#### en-us\GitHubCopilot.adml

3. After installing the policy and configure with the values in the example table above, you should see in settings by searching `@haspolicy otel` as follows:

title: Test: Copilot OTel Enterprise Policy
document:
Test: Copilot OTel Enterprise Policy

Refs: #323171

- [ ] anyOS @jruales
- [ ] anyOS @pwang347 

Complexity: 4

Authors: @zhichli

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23324133%0A%0A&assignees=zhichli)

---

_Companion TPI (MDM delivery): #324134_

### What it does

VS Code now consumes the GitHub Copilot `/copilot_internal/managed_settings` endpoint to apply **enterprise OpenTelemetry (OTel) policy** to both Copilot surfaces — the `github.copilot.chat.otel.*` extension settings and the `chat.agentHost.otel.*` agent-host settings — **without introducing a new settings namespace**. The managed `telemetry.*` block flows through the existing `AccountPolicyService` and respects the **Account Policy Gate** (the `ChatApprovedAccountOrganizations` allowlist).

---

### Prerequisites

> [!IMPORTANT]
> You need an **alternate GitHub account that is a member of the `VSCodeTesting` org** and seated for Copilot Business/Enterprise.
>
> **Ping @zhichli** if you need help

Sign out of your primary GitHub account in VS Code Insiders and sign in with the alt account.

The active policy lives in [`VSCodeTesting/.github-private/copilot/managed-settings.json`](https://github.com/VSCodeTesting/.github-private/blob/main/copilot/managed-settings.json) — **read it to know what's currently configured**, but don't edit it. You shouldn't need to edit it to get basic testing done.

### Supported keys

| Managed-setting key | Type | Example | Policy |
|---|---|---|---|
| `telemetry.enabled` | boolean | `true` | `CopilotOtelEnabled` |
| `telemetry.endpoint` | string (uri) | `http://localhost:4318` | `CopilotOtelEndpoint` |
| `telemetry.protocol` | string | `http/json` | `CopilotOtelProtocol`, `CopilotOtelOtlpProtocol` |
| `telemetry.captureContent` | boolean | `true` | `CopilotOtelCaptureContent` |
| `telemetry.lockCaptureContent` | boolean | `true` | `CopilotOtelCaptureContent` |
| `telemetry.serviceName` | string | `copilot-policy-smoke-test` | `CopilotOtelServiceName` |
| `telemetry.resourceAttributes` | object | `{"deployment.environment":"local","service.namespace":"vscode-testing-policy","policy.source":"managed-settings"}` | `CopilotOtelResourceAttributes` |
| `telemetry.headers` | object | `{"x-copilot-policy-test":"managed-settings","x-service-name":"copilot-policy-smoke-test"}` | `CopilotOtelHeaders` |

### Scenarios

Validate that the provided managed-keys configure the settings appropriately (use `@hasPolicy`):

_[`@hasPolicy` screenshot placeholder — to be added]_

#### 1. `telemetry.enabled` (`CopilotOtelEnabled`)

- [ ] With `telemetry.enabled: true` synced, both `github.copilot.chat.otel.enabled` and `chat.agentHost.otel.enabled` show the **"Managed by organization"** badge and are locked on.
- [ ] The user cannot turn OTel off via settings while it is managed.

#### 2. `telemetry.endpoint` / `telemetry.protocol` (`CopilotOtelEndpoint`, `CopilotOtelProtocol`, `CopilotOtelOtlpProtocol`)

- [ ] The OTLP endpoint and protocol settings are locked to the managed values on both surfaces.
- [ ] A managed endpoint/protocol **suppresses local file export** (the `outfile` diversion is disabled), so telemetry can't be redirected to a local file.

#### 3. `telemetry.captureContent` / `telemetry.lockCaptureContent` (`CopilotOtelCaptureContent`)

- [ ] A managed `captureContent` value is authoritative and shows the managed badge.
- [ ] With `lockCaptureContent: true`, the user **cannot enable** content capture themselves.

#### 4. `telemetry.serviceName` / `telemetry.resourceAttributes` / `telemetry.headers`

- [ ] `serviceName`, `resourceAttributes`, and `headers` reflect the managed values and show the managed badge.

---

### How to debug

**Developer: Policy Diagnostics** is the primary diagnostic. It shows the raw `managed_settings` bag, the gate state, last fetch status, and the applied policy values for each `CopilotOtel*` policy (and which channel — Server / Native MDM / File — is winning each key).

title: Bug Report: GitHub Copilot — "Response contained no choices" Error for ollama model
document:
Bug Report: GitHub Copilot — "Response contained no choices" Error for ollama model

# Bug Report: GitHub Copilot — "Response contained no choices" Error for ollama model

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.55.0
- VS Code Version: 1.127.0 (x64)
- OS Version: Windows Server 2019 Standard (NT 10.0.17763.0)
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): agent mode
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): ollama model
- Logs: (attach logs from Help > Toggle Developer Tools > Console)

[Sorry__your_request_failed__logs.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29615915/Sorry__your_request_failed__logs.json)

## Summary

Copilot chat fails with a non-recoverable error when the model returns a response with an empty `choices` array. The error is surfaced to the user as a generic "Sorry, your request failed. Please try again." message with no actionable guidance.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open VS Code with the GitHub Copilot Chat extension installed.
2. Submit a prompt in agent mode.
3. Observe the error message in the chat panel.

## Actual Behavior

The chat panel displays:

> Sorry, your request failed. Please try again.
> Client Request Id: 8d0b142c-fd40-4528-a781-68b9f8e072f3
> Reason: Response contained no choices.: Error: Response contained no choices.
> at qG._provideLanguageModelResponse (d:\Microsoft VS Code\4fe60c8b1c\resources\app\extensions\copilot\dist\extension.js:1690:14362)
> at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:104:5)
> at async qG.provideLanguageModelResponse (d:\Microsoft VS Code\4fe60c8b1c\resources\app\extensions\copilot\dist\extension.js:1690:15327)

The error is thrown inside `qG._provideLanguageModelResponse` in `extension.js` when the API response body has `choices: []` or omits the `choices` field entirely.

## Expected Behavior

- The extension should **handle an empty choices array gracefully** — e.g., by retrying, or surfacing a clear message such as:
 > "The model returned an empty response. Please try again."
- The raw JavaScript stack trace should **not** be exposed to the user.

## Impact

- Users lose the entire response with no output.
- The error message is confusing and provides no guidance on how to avoid or work around it.
- Re-trying the same prompt may or may not reproduce the issue (intermittent).

## Suggested Fix

In `_provideLanguageModelResponse`, guard against an empty or missing `choices` array before accessing `choices[0]`, and either retry the request or surface a user-friendly error without the internal stack trace.

## References

- Internal error location: `.\\resources\app\extensions\copilot\dist\extension.js` line 1690
- Related: VS Code Language Model API response streaming handling
- Related bug: "Response too long" error (`GitHub_Copilot_Bug_Response_Too_Long.md`) — same call site

title: GitHub Copilot — "Response too long" Error for ollama model
document:
GitHub Copilot — "Response too long" Error for ollama model

# Bug Report: GitHub Copilot — "Response too long" Error for ollama model

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.55.0
- VS Code Version: 1.127.0 (x64)
- OS Version: Windows Server 2019 Standard (NT 10.0.17763.0)
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): agent mode
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Ollama model
- Logs: (attach logs from Help > Toggle Developer Tools > Console)
[Response_too_long__logs.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29615860/Response_too_long__logs.json)

## Summary

Copilot chat fails with a non-recoverable error when the model generates a response that exceeds an internal length limit. The error is surfaced to the user as a generic "Sorry, your request failed. Please try again." message with no actionable guidance.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open VS Code with the GitHub Copilot Chat extension installed.
2. Submit a prompt that causes the model to generate a very long response (e.g., asking for a large file, a detailed explanation, or a complex code generation task).
3. Observe the error message in the chat panel.

## Actual Behavior

The chat panel displays:

> Sorry, your request failed. Please try again.
> Client Request Id: 02f844ef-bf8d-4e24-84e2-121b28b8a02d
> Reason: Response too long.: Error: Response too long.
> at qG._provideLanguageModelResponse (…extension.js:1690:14362)
> at async qG.provideLanguageModelResponse (…extension.js:1690:15327)

The error is thrown inside `qG._provideLanguageModelResponse` in `extension.js`.

## Expected Behavior

- The extension should **gracefully truncate** or **stream partial results** instead of throwing a hard error.
- Alternatively, the user should receive a **clear, actionable message** such as:
 > "The response was too long to complete. Try breaking your request into smaller parts."
- The raw JavaScript stack trace should **not** be exposed to the user.

## Impact

- Users lose the entire response with no partial output.
- The error message is confusing and provides no guidance on how to avoid it.
- Re-trying the same prompt will reproduce the same error.

## Suggested Fix

In `_provideLanguageModelResponse`, catch the `Response too long` condition before throwing and either:
1. Return the partial streamed response collected so far, or
2. Surface a user-friendly error message without the internal stack trace.

## References

- Internal error location: `.\\resources\app\extensions\copilot\dist\extension.js` line 1690
- Related: VS Code Language Model API response streaming handling

title: Virtual tool grouping silently drops tools mid-session; error blames the user ("currently disabled by the user")
document:
Virtual tool grouping silently drops tools mid-session; error blames the user ("currently disabled by the user")

## Summary

In a long agent session with a large toolset (~106 registered tools), the virtual tools packer recomputed its grouping at a turn boundary and dropped a tool from the request toolset entirely — it was neither top-level nor inside any `activate_*` virtual group. The same tool had been called successfully 12 times in the minutes immediately before. Subsequent calls failed with:

> Tool exo-run is currently disabled by the user, and cannot be called.

No user action had occurred (no Configure Tools interaction at all)

## Evidence

From the session's debug logs (`debug-logs//tools_N.json` snapshots plus `main.jsonl`):

| Snapshot | Time | `exo-run` status |
|---|---|---|
| `tools_0.json` | 20:25 | Present as a top-level tool; 12 successful `tool_call` events follow (20:49–20:53) |
| `tools_1.json` | 21:00 | **Absent from the entire payload** — `content.includes("exo-run") === false`. Not top-level, not in any virtual group, not even mentioned in group prose |
| `tools_2.json` | 21:04 | Still absent (mentioned only inside a sibling tool's description text) |

The `tools_0` / `tools_1` diff shows the repack was lossy in both directions: 5 tools dropped from the top level (`exo-run`, `exo-ping`, and three tools from a browser-automation extension), while 7 different tools from the same browser extension surfaced. A sibling tool (`exo-ping`) survived by being folded into an `activate_diagnostic_tools` group description — `exo-run` just vanished.

A second occurrence exists in the same session log: `tools_5.json` (previous day) is missing all five `exo-*` tools the same way.

Reproduced on: VS Code stable, macOS, default `github.copilot.chat.virtualTools.threshold` (not set by user). Notably, grouping was active at ~106 tools even though the setting's documented default threshold is 128.

## Problems

Needless to say, this is a pretty broken and confusing experience for both the agent and the user. The problems compound on each other.

1. **The repack loses tools outright.** A registered, enabled tool ended up neither top-level nor inside any virtual group after regrouping.
2. **Recency/usage does not pin tools.** The dropped tool was in active, successful use in the same session minutes before the repack.
3. **The error message misattributes the failure to the user.** "Currently disabled by the user" sent both me and the agent down a false debugging path (toggling tools in Configure Tools, running tool-reset commands, reloading). The actual state was "not present in this turn's assembled toolset."
4. **Each repack changes the serialized tool list**, which sits at the front of the prompt — invalidating provider-side prompt caches on every regroup even when no tools actually changed. With Anthropic-style prefix caching this is a full re-ingestion of the conversation.

## Suggested fixes

- Treat "successfully called this session" as a pinning signal so in-use tools survive regrouping.
- Guarantee the packer is lossless: every registered tool must be reachable either top-level or via some `activate_*` group.
- Reword the error to distinguish "tool not in this turn's toolset (regrouped/dropped)" from "disabled by the user."
- Consider stabilizing the grouping across turns within a session unless the underlying tool registrations actually change (also helps prompt-cache hit rates).

Happy to attach sanitized `tools_N.json` diffs and `main.jsonl` excerpts if they would be helpful.

title: Add a confirmation dialog with option to revert instead to "Git: Undo Last Commit" when last commit has been pushed
document:
Add a confirmation dialog with option to revert instead to "Git: Undo Last Commit" when last commit has been pushed

Git: Undo Last Commit does a soft reset, which is usually what a user wants when the commit has not yet been pushed, and often is _not_ what the user wants when the commit has been pushed (since it will then require force-pushing the new commit, which is typically only used by advanced users). VSCode should detect which of these situations it is in and suggest the appropriate action through a dialog.

title: Python notebook cell editor context menu is rendered behind the bottom panel on Windows
document:
Python notebook cell editor context menu is rendered behind the bottom panel on Windows

- VS Code Version: 1.127.0 (user setup)
- Commit: 4fe60c8b1cdac1c4c174f2fb180d0d758272d713
- Date: 2026-06-30T10:52:33+02:00
- Electron: 42.2.0
- ElectronBuildId: 14159160
- Chromium: 148.0.7778.97
- Node.js: 24.15.0
- V8: 14.8.178.14-electron.0
- OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200

**Steps to Reproduce:**

1. Open VS Code on Windows.
2. Open a Python notebook file, for example a `.ipynb` file.
3. Open the bottom panel where you can see `Terminal`, `Problems`, `Output`, and `Debug Console`, etc.
4. In the notebook editor, right-click on the code cell itself, outside the editable code text area.
5. Observe that the cell-level context menu is displayed correctly and is not blocked by the bottom panel.

Cell-level context menu is displayed correctly:

6 Then click inside the editable code area of the notebook cell.
7. Right-click on code/text inside the cell.
8. Observe that the code editor context menu appears, but the lower part of the menu is hidden behind the bottom panel. Options near the bottom of the code editor context menu, such as `Cut`, `Copy`, and `Paste`, cannot be seen or selected unless the bottom panel is closed, moved, or resized.

Lower menu is hidden behind the bottom panel:

All menu options are visible after resizing the bottom panel:

**Expected behavior:**

Both notebook context menus should be rendered above the bottom panel. The context menu for editing code inside a notebook cell should remain fully visible and selectable, just like the cell-level context menu.

**Actual behavior:**

On Windows, the cell-level context menu appears correctly. However, the context menu shown when right-clicking inside the editable code area of a notebook cell is partially rendered behind the bottom panel. This makes lower menu options such as `Cut`, `Copy`, and `Paste` blocked and inaccessible.

**Additional information:**

- This issue appears to be specific to the notebook cell editor context menu on Windows. In a regular Python file, such as a `.py` file, right-clicking on code shows the code editor context menu correctly, and the menu is not blocked by the bottom panel.

Menu options are visible in a regular `.py` file:

- Please also note that this issue does not affect the cell-level context menu in notebooks, as mentioned above. If I right-click on the notebook code cell itself, outside the editable code text area, the cell-level context menu is displayed correctly. The problem only happens when I right-click inside the editable code area of a notebook cell.

- A similar issue was previously investigated here: (https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/184455), but it appears the verification was done only on macOS. The issue still persists on Windows.

**Workaround:**

Closing, moving, or resizing the bottom panel makes the full code editor context menu visible. However, the code editor context menu inside notebook cells should not be blocked by the bottom panel in the first place.

Thank you for looking into this. I appreciate the maintainers’ work on VS Code. This is my first time opening an issue here, so please let me know if you need any additional information.

title: AutoApprove rules are not honored under Default Approvals for terminal commands
document:
AutoApprove rules are not honored under Default Approvals for terminal commands

# `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove` rules are not honored under "Default Approvals" for terminal commands like `git diff`

Summary

When Copilot Chat is in "Default Approvals" mode, terminal commands that are explicitly allowed by `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove` still show the "Run pwsh command?" confirmation dialog instead of auto-running.

This makes `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove` ineffective for the expected user scenario: allowing safe terminal commands without repeated prompts.

Environment

- VS Code: 1.127.0
- GitHub Copilot Chat / Copilot: 0.55.0
- OS: Windows 10 Pro
- Shell: PowerShell
- Chat permission mode: "Default Approvals"

Step 1. Configure `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove` with an allow rule for `git diff`, for example:

Step 2. In Copilot Chat, keep the permission mode set to "Default Approvals".

Step 3. Ask Copilot Chat to run this command:

Actual Result

A confirmation dialog appears:

- "Run pwsh command?"
- User must click "Allow" manually

Expected Result

Because the command matches an explicit allow rule in `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove`, it should auto-run without showing the confirmation dialog while in "Default Approvals".

Additional Notes

- The command is safe/read-only and is exactly the kind of command users expect to whitelist via `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove`.
- The command runs successfully after manual approval, so the problem is not command validity.
- From a user perspective, "Default Approvals" appears not to actually "use configured rules" for terminal commands in the expected way.
- This seems like either a regression or a behavior change that makes `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove` much less useful than before.
- `Bypass Approvals` is not an acceptable workaround, because it auto-approves all tool calls, not only the commands explicitly allowed in `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove`.

Why this is a problem

Users configure `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove` specifically to avoid repeated prompts for safe commands such as `git diff`, `git status`, etc. If those commands still trigger the top-level approval dialog in "Default Approvals", the setting no longer provides the expected value.

title: Codex app-server crashes with SIGTRAP on any Agent task after updating VS Code to 1.127.0 (Intel macOS Monterey)
document:
Codex app-server crashes with SIGTRAP on any Agent task after updating VS Code to 1.127.0 (Intel macOS Monterey)

Environment
VS Code: 1.127.0
ChatGPT Extension: openai.chatgpt

Codex CLI (bundled by extension): Native macos-x86_64
Machine: MacBook Air (Early 2015)
CPU: Intel Core i5-5350U
Architecture: x86_64

Problem

After updating VS Code to 1.127.0, the ChatGPT extension's Codex Agent consistently crashes whenever it starts performing any repository-related task.

The extension loads successfully, authentication works, and normal chat functions correctly.

However, as soon as the Agent begins interacting with the workspace (for example, inspecting files, reading the repository, or creating a file), the bundled Codex app-server terminates unexpectedly.

Open VS Code 1.127.0.
Open any repository (also reproducible in a brand-new empty folder).
Open the ChatGPT extension.
Start a Codex Agent session.
Ask the agent to perform any workspace task such as:
"Inspect this repository."
"Read package.json."
"Create a README.md."
Observe that the Codex process crashes immediately.
Expected Behavior

The Codex Agent should inspect the repository and complete the requested task.

Actual Behavior

The native Codex process exits unexpectedly.

The extension reports:

Codex process errored:
Codex app-server process exited unexpectedly
(code=unknown, signal=SIGTRAP)

The last CLI message before the crash is:

codex_rollout::list:
state db discrepancy during find_thread_path_by_id_str_in_subdir:
falling_back

After the crash, the extension repeatedly reports:

Codex process is not available
Attempted to send app-server message but stdin is destroyed
Troubleshooting Already Performed

I have already verified the following:

Recreated ~/.codex from scratch.
Tested with multiple unrelated repositories.
Tested with a completely empty workspace.
Reinstalled the ChatGPT extension.
Downgraded the ChatGPT extension to a previous version.
Restarted VS Code multiple times.
Verified the correct Intel (macos-x86_64) Codex binary is being launched.

The issue persists in all cases.

Crash Report

The macOS crash report shows the native codex process terminating with:

EXC_BREAKPOINT (SIGTRAP)

The fault occurs during V8 initialization:

The crash occurs before any repository analysis appears to begin

title: Allow Repository inheritence of MCP Servers like Skills
document:
Allow Repository inheritence of MCP Servers like Skills

Right now you have to repeat yourself for every workspace within a repo (especially painful with a mono-repro). It would be good if we had an option similar to chat.useCustomizationsInParentRepositories that allowed yo uto specify that it will walk the stack and look for mcp servers up to the top of the repro not just what's in the workspace. This also has issues because of multi-root workspace files which have no "root" to look in.

Ideally this would walk upwards looking for both .mcp.json and the vs code specific one (but you should just standardize on .mcp.json) and add them all together to create all of the mcps available. This would look in every folder in the workspace file, and then in the same folder as the workspace file, and then all parent folders to the root of the repo.

If just opening a folder, it would look in the current and walk upwards obviously.

This eliminates the need for duplicate .mcp.json files everywhere.

title: On 'manageCheckboxStateManually', refreshing node with new checkbox state flips all parent checkbox states recursively
document:
On 'manageCheckboxStateManually', refreshing node with new checkbox state flips all parent checkbox states recursively

# Background 

I am working on a custom vsocde extension. This extension uses a `treeView` and a `treeDataProvider`. Each node has a checkbox which I have to manually update (`manageCheckboxStateManually` is set to `true`), as I want the following behaviour, which differs from the default behaviour :
- Children are always update with the new state of the target clicked
- Parents are always checked, as long as one of its children is checked.

Attached is a minimum working example, based on the `vscode-tree-view` sample. In this example, I am keeping track of the checkbox states of all nodes via a simple map. 

# Observed

Now, when I uncheck an item, all of its parent items checkbox state gets set to `unchecked`, even though my code clearly doesn't set the checkbox state to `unchecked.` All parents are update even though, only the affected parents are given to `onDidChangeTreeData.fire` event, here the `refresh` method.

Similar thing seems to be happening when checking but not sure

# Expected

I would expected vscode to just update the node and its children which I provided to the refresh method. Additionally, I would expecte the parent checkboxes to not get flipped/changed.

# Steps to reproduce

1. Open the attached example. 
2. `npm install`
3. Run the extension with F5 and navigate to the `Test View`
4. Check all checkboxes of node `a`
5. Expanded node `aa`
6. Now unchecked `aaa`, `aab`, and finally `aac`.
7. After unchecking the last node, both `aa` and `a` will be unchecked, even though only `aa` should be unchecked.

An even better example is to expand the complete tree and just uncheck `aaaaaa`. This will trigger a recursive update of the parents and its checkbox states.

# Potential Workaround

It seems like, when I pass the parent of the node that changed, meaning a node that in theory didn't change, then it seems to work? To me, this doesn't make much sense but maybe I am overlooking something and it is an error on my side?

# Attachments
[checkboxTest.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29593677/checkboxTest.zip)

# Further info

This function handles the checkbox change. `affectedKeys` holds all nodes that changed and must be update.
First, all children/descendants are updated via the `setDescendants` function
Second, we travers upwards the parents/ancerstors and break early, if the parent checkbox state did not change.
Then we pass the affectedKeys to the refresh. We could theoretically also just pass the highest parent here but doesn't matter for the example.
To add the workaround comment in `affectedKeys.add(ancestorKey);`.

 

# Environment

title: Copy button in Copilot Chat includes unrelated text from previous user message
document:
Copy button in Copilot Chat includes unrelated text from previous user message

In a Copilot Chat session, send a message that starts with a word or phrase (e.g., "Updated todo list") followed by pasted content.
Receive a response from Copilot.
Click the built-in Copy button on any subsequent Copilot response in the same session.
Paste the copied text.
Expected: Only the Copilot response text is copied.

Actual: The copied text includes an additional string ("Updated todo list") prepended or appended, originating from a previous user message in the same session.

title: NES xtab: gather language context and neighbor snippets concurrently instead of sequentially
document:
NES xtab: gather language context and neighbor snippets concurrently instead of sequentially

In `XtabProvider.provideNextEdit` (`extensions/copilot/src/extension/xtab/node/xtabProvider.ts`), the two context-gathering steps are awaited one after the other:

`gatherLanguageContext()` and `gatherNeighborSnippets()` are independent, so awaiting them sequentially adds their latencies together on the critical path before the prompt can be built. They should be started together (e.g. `Promise.all`) so they run concurrently.

Notes for whoever picks this up:
- This pattern appears in both the global-budget branch and the legacy/prod `else` branch, so both should be updated.
- Preserve the existing cancellation checks and their distinct `GotCancelled` reasons (`afterLanguageContextAwait`, `afterNeighborSnippetsAwait`) for telemetry continuity, checking cancellation after the combined await.
- Preserve the `nLinesOfCurrentFileInPrompt` telemetry timing in the prod branch (the current-file clip happens before these awaits there).

Surfaced during review of #323948.

title: When utility small model is not selected, Copilot fails to use BYOK provider
document:
When utility small model is not selected, Copilot fails to use BYOK provider

related to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/322751: Idea is good, but implementation is flawed: currently if no utility model selected, Copilot just fails to respond, showing the error

> No utility model is configured for 'copilot-utility-small' while the selected main model is BYOK.

Moreover, I'm using llama.cpp with the model fitted tightly to VRAM, so re-loading the model is much higher overhead than just using the currently selected one for small requests. But if I have Qwen3.6 selected, and my utility model is, for example, Gemma 4 12B, its usage will require re-loading the model. To avoid that, there should be an option "Use same model as the main selected one", and a way to select models of a different provider (currently, dropdowns for utility models only list models of the BYOK provider).

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: builtin to VS Code
- VS Code Version: 1.128.0-insider (7e7f9505b9ef0a7a0899794997d4b1cb608084fc)
- OS Version: 10.0.26200.8655
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): BYOK
- Logs:

1. Configure `chatLanguageModels.json` to include

2. Open Agentic panel, select Qwen3.6-28B-REAP20-A3B-256k
3. Say "Hi", Enter

you'll get the error

> No utility model is configured for 'copilot-utility-small' while the selected main model is BYOK.

4. Configure `unsloth_gemma-4-12b-it-GGUF:Q6_K_XL-256k` in vscode-insiders://settings/chat.utilitySmallModel
5. Retry the response

You'll see the local engine will load the second model (along with the first one if limit permits, or will unload the first model) for the utility use, then switch back to the first model, which is much slower than just generating everything with the same main model.

You may ask, why would I select qwen while gemma is configured as the utility? I frequently switch between models, including a few of my BYOK and models provided by Copilot.

The previous behavior of always using copilot models for utility worked for me mostly, but it is flawed in some parts and for example prevented me from ever using commit message generation with copilot. Also it fails when internet connectivity is missing. This in an opportunity to fix both issues!

Also perhaps, the utility model should be configurable per provider in `chatLanguageModels.json`, with same options as available in the user `settings.json`, so a cloud BYOK configuration allow setting up different `chat.utilityModel` and `chat.utilitySmallModel`, while llama.cpp config having `main` there (or some other non-model-name keyword) to avoid re-loading.

Either way, utility models should not be restricted to the same provider as the main selected model.

title: Plan mode re-sends the "Start with Auto Copilot" command after switching back from Agent mode, causing duplicate execution and extra credit consumption
document:
Plan mode re-sends the "Start with Auto Copilot" command after switching back from Agent mode, causing duplicate execution and extra credit consumption

## Description

I found a reproducible issue when using the Plan and Agent workflow.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Copilot Chat.
2. Switch to **Plan** mode.
3. Ask Copilot to generate an implementation plan.
4. After the plan is generated, click **Start with Auto Copilot**.
5. Wait until Auto Copilot finishes executing all tasks.
6. Switch from **Agent** mode back to **Plan** mode.

## Expected Behavior

Switching back to Plan mode should simply display the completed plan and allow me to continue planning or make changes. No additional action should be triggered automatically.

## Actual Behavior

As soon as I switch back to Plan mode, Copilot automatically sends the previous **"Start with Auto Copilot"** implementation request again, causing the implementation process to be triggered a second time.

This happens even though the previous Auto Copilot task has already completed successfully.

## Impact

This issue has two negative effects:

* The implementation is executed twice unnecessarily.
* The unintended second execution also consumes additional AI credits.

Because the second execution is triggered automatically when switching back to Plan mode, there is no opportunity to cancel it beforehand. This can lead to unnecessary credit usage and may quickly consume a user's available quota.

## Environment

* VS Code Version: 1.127.0 (Universal)
* Commit: 4fe60c8b1cdac1c4c174f2fb180d0d758272d713
* Date: 2026-06-30T10:52:33+02:00
* Electron: 42.2.0
* Electron Build ID: 14159160
* Chromium: 148.0.7778.97
* Node.js: 24.15.0
* V8: 14.8.178.14-electron.0
* OS: macOS 13.6 (Darwin x64 22.6.0)

## Additional Notes

It appears that switching back to Plan mode replays or re-executes the previous **"Start with Auto Copilot"** action instead of simply restoring the completed planning session.

In my opinion, switching between **Plan** and **Agent** modes should not automatically trigger any new execution. It should only restore the previous conversation state unless the user explicitly starts a new Auto Copilot run.

I can provide a screen recording if that would help reproduce the issue.

title: Copilot Chat: first invocation of a remote MCP tool fails with "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'invoke')"
document:
Copilot Chat: first invocation of a remote MCP tool fails with "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'invoke')"

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.55.0
- VS Code Version: 1.127.0 (also reproduced on Insiders 1.128.0-insider)
- OS Version: Linux (Ubuntu-based) x64
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent mode, tool invocation of a remote MCP server (GitHub MCP Server, `mcp_github_mcp_se_*` tools)
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude Sonnet 5
- Logs: see excerpt below

### Description

The first time a **remote MCP tool** is invoked right after a window/session becomes active (extension host activation, new login, or after "Workspace folders changed"), the call fails with:

Built-in tools (`read_file`, `run_in_terminal`, etc.) are **not** affected — only MCP-server-provided tools fail on this first call. Subsequent calls to the same tool a few seconds later succeed without any user action.

1. Configure a remote MCP server (e.g. GitHub MCP Server) so its tools show up in the agent's tool list.
2. Open a new window / re-activate the extension host (e.g. reload window, or let it sit idle until it re-authenticates).
3. In the very first agent turn that calls a tool provided by that MCP server, the call fails with the `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'invoke')` shown above.
4. Ask the same thing again (or wait ~5-10s) — the same MCP tool now invokes successfully.

### Log excerpt (timeline)

### Analysis

It looks like the MCP tool's schema/entry becomes visible in the agent's tool list before the underlying invocation handler (the object whose `.invoke` is being read) has finished being wired up for that server connection. The tool list is populated slightly ahead of the connection being fully ready, so the very first invocation attempt hits a race where the handler object is still `undefined`.

This has been reproduced consistently across multiple sessions, always as the *first* MCP tool call after the extension host / MCP connection becomes active, and always resolves itself on the next attempt.

### Expected Behavior

The tool list should only expose an MCP tool for invocation once its handler is fully initialized, or `invokeTool` should await/retry the connection instead of throwing a `TypeError` when the handler is momentarily `undefined`.

### Workaround

- Wait a few seconds / send a non-tool message first after opening a new window before invoking any MCP-provided tool.
- Retry the same request; the second attempt succeeds.

title: Agent Host: tool responses with long outputs create an extremely wide file
document:
Agent Host: tool responses with long outputs create an extremely wide file

Tool responses with long outputs are saved into a file with just 6 lines, but the 4th line is extremely long (68K character long) with literal newline characters, instead of a multi-line file. This forces the agent to use Python to parse the JSON and make sense of it:

Maybe for text outputs we should just have the text itself as a regular multi-line file instead of turning the file into a single-line literal string inside a JSON file? That could make it easier for the agent to parse the file contents via simpler methods that don't require Python, such as terminal tools.

Attaching the file shown in the screenshot above in case it helps:

title: Track full session porting for Continue in Agent Host
document:
Track full session porting for Continue in Agent Host

Related: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/315193
Related attachment tracking issue: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/323969
Related scoped PR: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/323773

## Context

PR #323773 handles a narrow attachment case: when a user attaches a Copilot CLI session from Agent Host chat, Agent Host can forward the session's `events.jsonl` as context.

This issue tracks a different problem: full session porting for `continue in` flows. In this path, the user is not merely attaching a previous session via the `+`/context UI. Instead, the old session should become, or be imported into, the active Agent Host conversation.

## Attachment vs porting

| Flow | User intent | Mechanism | Current PR #323773? |
|---|---|---|---:|
| Session attachment | Reference another session as context in the current chat | Visible session chip plus model-readable attachment, currently `events.jsonl` for Copilot CLI | Yes, only for Copilot CLI `events.jsonl` sessions |
| Full session port / `continue in` | Continue/resume an existing session in Agent Host | Convert/import/resume the previous session as the active Agent Host conversation | No |

## Porting questions to resolve

- Which source session types should be portable into Agent Host?
- What is the canonical source of truth for each source session type?
 - Copilot CLI `events.jsonl`
 - `IChatModel` / workbench chat model state
 - provider-specific session content
 - AHP session state
- How should tool calls, tool results, file edits, approvals, working directory, model selection, and session metadata map into the target Agent Host session?
- Should porting preserve original session identity or create a new Agent Host session linked back to the source?
- What should happen when source session data is partial, provider-specific, or unavailable?

## Initial matrix

| Source session type | Example URI | Possible source of truth | Porting status |
|---|---|---|---|
| EH Copilot CLI | `copilotcli:/` | `~/.copilot/session-state//events.jsonl` | Needs design |
| Local Agent Host Copilot CLI | `agent-host-copilotcli:/` | AHP state plus Copilot CLI `events.jsonl` | Needs design |
| Remote Agent Host Copilot CLI | `remote--copilotcli:/` | Remote AHP state plus remote `events.jsonl` | Needs design |
| Other Agent Host/AHP sessions | provider-specific Agent Host session URI | AHP state/provider state | Needs design |
| Local/workbench chat sessions | workbench chat session URI | `IChatModel` / chat session model | Needs design |
| Claude Code / other providers | provider-specific URI | Provider-specific session state | Needs design |

## Non-goal

This issue does not track using a previous session as an attachment/context item. That matrix is tracked in #323969.

/cc @connor4312 @roblourens

title: Track session-reference attachment support across chat session types
document:
Track session-reference attachment support across chat session types

Related: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/315193
Related PR: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/323773

## Context

PR #323773 is intentionally scoped to Agent Host session references that can resolve to a Copilot CLI `events.jsonl` file. That gives Agent Host Copilot a concrete model-readable attachment source without introducing a new AHP attachment kind or attempting broader session migration.

This issue tracks the broader attachment matrix for session references across chat/session providers.

## Current scoped behavior in #323773

- Agent Host-backed chats only show attachable Copilot CLI sessions in the `Sessions...` picker.
- Supported session references are converted into:
 - a visible session chip (`Simple` attachment) for UI/replay metadata
 - a hidden `Resource` attachment pointing at the host-local `events.jsonl`
- Unsupported session references are not converted as model-readable attachments by Agent Host.

## Session attachment matrix

| Referenced session type | Example URI | Readable history source | Supported by #323773? | Follow-up path |
|---|---|---|---:|---|
| EH Copilot CLI | `copilotcli:/` | Local `~/.copilot/session-state//events.jsonl` | Yes | Keep using `events.jsonl` as attachment source |
| Local Agent Host Copilot CLI | `agent-host-copilotcli:/` | Local `~/.copilot/session-state//events.jsonl` | Yes | Keep using `events.jsonl` as attachment source |
| Remote Agent Host Copilot CLI | `remote--copilotcli:/` | Remote host `~/.copilot/session-state//events.jsonl`, when connection/home resolves | Yes, when resolvable | Keep using host-local `events.jsonl` path |
| Other Agent Host/AHP sessions | provider-specific Agent Host session URI | No shared `events.jsonl` contract | No | Need first-class AHP session-reference support or an `IChatModel`/session-content path |
| Claude Code sessions | provider-specific session URI | Not Copilot CLI `events.jsonl` | No | Need provider-specific or common session model access |
| Local/workbench chat sessions | workbench chat session URI | `IChatModel`/chat session model, not Copilot CLI `events.jsonl` | No | Need `IChatModel`/session-content attachment path |
| Other extension-host chat sessions | extension/provider-specific URI | Provider-specific, no shared `events.jsonl` contract | No | Need provider-specific or first-class session-reference contract |

## Open questions

- Should session references become a first-class AHP attachment concept instead of being represented through `Simple` + `Resource`?
- Should non-Copilot-CLI sessions expose model-readable attachment content through `IChatModel`, `IChatSessionsService`, or provider-specific session content APIs?
- What UI affordance should distinguish a visible session chip from hidden model-readable context resources?

## Non-goal

This issue is about attaching a referenced session as context. Full session migration/porting for `continue in` should be tracked separately.

/cc @connor4312 @roblourens

title: .gitignore will prevent an extension from launching.
document:
.gitignore will prevent an extension from launching.

- VS Code Version: 1.127
- OS Version: windows 10

When an extension uses workspaceContains as an activation event and the path uses global wildcard with a folder, .gitignore with a certain glob pattern will prevent the extension from launching.

It seems that workspaceContains search respects the .gitignore on some edge cases and uses Use Ignore Files setting (on by default) so the file cannot be found, and the extension will fail to launch. I would assume that this is not intended behavior?

project structure:

1. git has been initialized and Use Ignore Files enabled
2. extension has "activationEvents": [ "workspaceContains:**/Content/test123.json"]
3. test123.json inside Content folder
4. **/Content or /Content inside .gitignore

however, with **/Content/* inside .gitignore this will work and the extension is launched.

This also works (extension will launch with these settings):
setting in package.json: "activationEvents": [ "workspaceContains:Content/test123.json"]
test123.json or **/Content inside .gitignore

title: Unable to copy or capture whole chat session content.
document:
Unable to copy or capture whole chat session content.

The following report was generated by Visual Studio Code Ask Agent.

Here is a concise internal engineering report you can send:

Subject: Internal Engineering Report — Inability to Capture Full Chat Session Content and Transcript-Generation Loop

Summary:
During a recent Copilot chat session, the user reported inability to capture the full chat session content through the available chat UI controls. Attempts to use “Copy all”, “Copy conversation”, and “Export chat” were unsuccessful or ineffective. In addition, when asked to generate a clean transcript of the entire session, the assistant entered a repetitive response loop and required a stop command to terminate the behavior.

Observed Behavior:

The chat UI did not provide a reliable mechanism to copy or export the full session content.
Ctrl+A did not select the complete chat history as expected.
Copying only captured a partial segment of the conversation rather than the whole session.
Transcript generation repeatedly re-emitted similar content rather than producing a one-shot full transcript.
Impact:

Users cannot reliably export or copy complete chat sessions for documentation, review, debugging, or support purposes.
The transcript-generation behavior can create a poor user experience and appears to be unstable under certain prompts.
Requested Action:

Investigate and improve whole-session copy/export support in the chat interface.
Investigate the repetitive transcript-generation behavior and implement a guard against looping or repeated content generation.
Consider adding a reliable export path for full conversation history.

title: Audit deprecated Copilot SDK terminal content usage
document:
Audit deprecated Copilot SDK terminal content usage

Follow-up from https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/323922 and https://github.com/github/copilot-sdk/issues/1803.

Copilot SDK now exposes `shell_exit` for shell command exit metadata, and SDK `terminal` content is deprecated for that purpose. I want to make sure we do not keep depending on SDK `type: "terminal"` content for new shell status behavior.

From a quick audit, the Agent Host `ToolResultContentType.Terminal` usages are not the same thing as the deprecated SDK terminal content. Those are VS Code/AHP terminal resource references with a terminal URI, and should stay separate from SDK `shell_exit` metadata.

Actual deprecated SDK terminal-content dependency found:

- `formatSearchToolInvocationCompleted` still reads SDK `result.contents[0].type === 'terminal'` and `text` for glob/grep/rg result parsing:
 - https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/9d925988ae356620214d52776fba0a4bb486fe1f/extensions/copilot/src/extension/chatSessions/copilotcli/common/copilotCLITools.ts#L1267-L1268
- Tests still encode that legacy SDK `terminal` content shape for glob/grep/rg:
 - https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/9d925988ae356620214d52776fba0a4bb486fe1f/extensions/copilot/src/extension/chatSessions/copilotcli/common/test/copilotCLITools.spec.ts#L912-L982

Places checked that are probably not deprecation debt:

- Agent Host custom terminal content uses `ToolResultContentType.Terminal` as a live terminal resource reference (`resource`, `title`), not SDK terminal output text:
 - https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/9d925988ae356620214d52776fba0a4bb486fe1f/src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/copilot/copilotAgentSession.ts#L637-L640
 - https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/9d925988ae356620214d52776fba0a4bb486fe1f/src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/copilot/copilotAgentSession.ts#L2672-L2677
- Chat-side terminal rendering reads Agent Host terminal resource blocks by URI, not deprecated SDK terminal output blocks:
 - https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/9d925988ae356620214d52776fba0a4bb486fe1f/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentHost/stateToProgressAdapter.ts#L144-L150
 - https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/9d925988ae356620214d52776fba0a4bb486fe1f/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentHost/stateToProgressAdapter.ts#L718-L722

Work:

- Confirm whether the internal Copilot CLI extension path should keep supporting SDK `terminal` content for glob/grep/rg result parsing as legacy compatibility.
- If not, migrate search result parsing to `result.content` or another non-deprecated SDK content shape.
- Keep `shell_exit` limited to shell command exit metadata; do not treat it as display output or as a live terminal resource.
- Rename or update tests so any remaining SDK `terminal` coverage is clearly marked as legacy compatibility.
- Make sure Agent Host `ToolResultContentType.Terminal` remains documented as a VS Code/AHP terminal resource, not deprecated SDK terminal content.

This is mostly cleanup/debt after adopting `shell_exit`; I do not think it blocks the command decoration fix.

I lost hours of my work just because the website closed my chat without my permission.

I spent hours working on some code for my personal use to help with my studies, and when it was practically finished, the chat just closed, and I lost work that took almost the ENTIRE DAY. Then, I tried to get the AI to find where the chat went, or at least recover the code and the implementation plan. The AI gave several useless answers and only became helpful in looking for what I needed after I pressured it a lot. Because of this, just when it was perhaps finally finding my code, my token limit was reached.

This is absurd and incredibly disrespectful. My work was lost because the chat closed on its own and can't be found anywhere. So, ENTIRELY DUE TO THE SITE'S FAULT, I had to waste more tokens trying to recover my work, the limit maxed out, and I still didn't get my work back. It is absurd that the site allows data to be lost this way, but since it did happen, the absolute minimum would be not cutting off my tokens so I could at least recover what I had done—which is already a frustrating hassle that is not my fault.

If no action is taken, this will be the first and last time I use this website. I wasted my entire day for NOTHING!"
