title: benchmark: comparar consumo de tokens entre CLI e Code7 (agent host)
document:
benchmark: comparar consumo de tokens entre CLI e Code7 (agent host)

## Objetivo

Comparar o custo real de tokens por turno entre o uso direto do `claude` CLI e o uso via Code7 (agent host / claude-agent-sdk), rodando as mesmas prompts nos dois ambientes.

## Motivação

O Code7 envia um system prompt maior (plugins, skills, agent customizations) que pode encarecer o custo por sessão. Com prompt caching, o impacto se dilui após o primeiro turno — mas precisa ser medido para confirmar se vale mudar o modelo padrão de `claude-opus-4-8` para `claude-sonnet-4-6`.

## Como coletar os dados

### Code7
- Habilitar `chat.agentHost.ipcLoggingEnabled: true`
- Cada turno emite um evento `session/usage` nos arquivos `.jsonl` em `~/.config/VS Code7/logs/*/ahp/`
- Campos: `inputTokens`, `cacheReadTokens`, `outputTokens`, `model`

### CLI
- Rodar prompts via `claude --print ""` (modo não-interativo)
- Capturar uso via `--output-format json` ou via logs do `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` isolado

## Plano de implementação sugerido

1. Definir um conjunto fixo de N prompts (ex: 10) de complexidade variada
2. Rodar as N prompts no CLI (`claude --print`) e capturar token usage
3. Rodar as mesmas N prompts no Code7 via IPC e capturar `session/usage`
4. Medir pelo menos 5–10 turnos por ambiente para capturar o comportamento pós-cache (não só o cold start)
5. Calcular custo estimado por turno com os preços atuais de cada modelo
6. Gerar relatório comparativo: CLI vs Code7, cold start vs cache aquecido

## Critérios de sucesso

- [ ] Script automatizado que roda as prompts nos dois ambientes
- [ ] Relatório com custo médio por turno (pós-cache) por ambiente
- [ ] Conclusão sobre se vale mudar o modelo padrão para Sonnet 4.6

title: Add fixed OAuth callback port option for MCP servers in Copilot
document:
Add fixed OAuth callback port option for MCP servers in Copilot

### Feature Request: Add fixed OAuth callback port option for MCP servers in Copilot

#### Problem

When connecting to a remote MCP server that requires OAuth authentication, VS Code Copilot binds to a random ephemeral port for the localhost callback (e.g. `http://localhost:54321/callback`). The MCP spec states that authorization servers *SHOULD* accept any loopback port, but many identity providers require the exact `redirect_uri` (including port) to be pre-registered.

There are a couple reasons this is not always practical:

- Enterprise identity providers (Cognito, Entra ID, Okta in strict mode) enforce exact redirect URI matching as a security policy
- Organizations with compliance requirements may not be able to relax redirect URI validation

#### Proposed Solution

Allow users to specify a fixed callback port in the MCP server's `oauth` configuration:

When `callbackPort` is set, the OAuth callback listener binds to that specific port instead of an ephemeral one, producing a predictable redirect URI (e.g. `http://localhost:8080/callback`) that can be pre-registered with the identity provider.

#### Other Client Examples

This pattern has been adopted by a handful of clients already:

- **Claude Code** — [`--callback-port` flag and `"callbackPort"` in JSON config](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp#use-a-fixed-oauth-callback-port)
- **Kiro CLI** — [`"redirectUri"` field in oauth config](https://kiro.dev/docs/cli/mcp/#mcpjson-file)
- **Codex** — [`mcp_oauth_callback_port` in config.toml](https://developers.openai.com/codex/mcp#configure-with-configtoml)

title: CoPilot Chat
document:
CoPilot Chat

I had my Copilot credits reset today and I was excited to get back to work. My first task was to fix a regression bug that my Copilot agent introduced. 

I started a fresh chat (As recommended online). I asked the agent to implement a simple fix. 5 minutes later I had use 25% of my monthly credits. I had selected the so-called "10% cheaper" auto AI agent. 

After a lonnnnggg profanity laced scream, I kept working and the token usage didn't improve. In less than 1 hour, I used 36% of my monthly AI tokens. A token policy that gave me 40 hours / month of AI agent now gives me <4 hours.

This is not an acceptable cost for what is delivered. I am freelance and I am not going to spend insane money so I can clumsily create sub-par apps with AI agents that constantly break my code, regardless of how many requirements and regression tests I define. (I call it regressive AI)

If this is the new normal, please return my money. It is time for me to retire.

title: Extension host crash loop on Stable 1.122.1: built-in Copilot Chat 0.50.1 registers undeclared "claude-code" chatParticipant → TypeError: e is not iterable
document:
Extension host crash loop on Stable 1.122.1: built-in Copilot Chat 0.50.1 registers undeclared "claude-code" chatParticipant → TypeError: e is not iterable

- VS Code Version: 1.122.1 (commit 8761a5560cfd65fdd19ce7e2bd18dab5c0a4d84e), arm64
- OS Version: macOS 15.7.4 (24G517), Apple Silicon

1. Use the latest Stable VS Code 1.122.1 (which bundles built-in `GitHub.copilot-chat` **0.50.1**) on a setup where the Copilot Chat "Claude agent" path is exercised at startup (a `claude-code` chat participant gets registered).
2. Open a window; the extension host activates `GitHub.copilot-chat`.
3. Within seconds the extension host crashes and respawns, looping until VS Code shows: **"Extension host terminated unexpectedly 3 times within the last 5 minutes."**

## What happens

Every extension-host cycle logs two errors, then the host dies with **exit code 5**, respawns, and repeats.

`exthost.log`:

`main.log`:

The `TypeError: e is not iterable` is thrown from inside a `setTimeout` callback (`kP.init` → `setItems`), so it is an **unhandled** exception that terminates the whole extension host. The host respawns, re-runs the same init, and crashes again → 3 crashes in 5 minutes. This is the fatal variant, not just the log line referenced in #276860.

## Root cause (diagnosed)

The built-in `GitHub.copilot-chat` **v0.50.1** is internally inconsistent:

- `extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js` references `claude-code` (6 occurrences, plus a `ClaudeWorkspaceFolderService`) and attempts to register a `claude-code` chat participant.
- `extensions/copilot/package.json` declares only `github.copilot.*` participants (`github.copilot.default`, `github.copilot.editsAgent`, `github.copilot.terminal`, etc.) — **`claude-code` is not declared.**

So `$registerAgent` rejects the undeclared `claude-code` participant; the agent/items collection is then left in a state that makes `setItems(e)` receive a non-iterable value, and the unhandled throw kills the extension host.

## Things that do NOT work around it

- `"github.copilot.chat.claudeAgent.enabled": false` — no effect (the participant is still registered and the crash still happens).
- `"chat.useAgentSkills": false` — no effect.
- Disabling third-party extensions — no effect (the crasher is a built-in extension).
- Downgrading the extension is refused: `Extension 'github.copilot-chat' is a built-in extension with version '0.50.1' and cannot be downgraded`.
- 1.122.1 is the latest Stable, so there is no Stable update to move to.

## What fixes it

VS Code **Insiders** bundles `GitHub.copilot-chat` **0.51.2026060101**. With that build the `claude-code` error appears at most once and is **non-fatal** — the extension host does not enter the crash loop (0 `crashed with code 5` events across multiple sessions in `main.log`). So the fix appears to already be in the 0.51 line; the problem is that the broken **0.50.1** is welded into the latest **Stable** (1.122.1) as a built-in and cannot be replaced without changing the VS Code build.

## Request

Ship a Stable patch (e.g. 1.122.2) bundling a Copilot Chat build that either declares the `claude-code` chat participant or guards the `setItems` iteration / catches the registration failure, so Stable users on 1.122.1 are not stuck in an extension-host crash loop. Reopening / cross-linking #276860 may also help, since it is closed but still being reported.

title: Model only visible when response completes in auto mode
document:
Model only visible when response completes in auto mode

In auto model selection mode, if you hover over the response, it tells you the model that was used. But it only does that after the response fully completes. I'd like to see it while the response is generating (ideally right after the prompt is sent or as soon as the model is known).

This may not be necessary as I gain trust in auto model selection, but as I'm starting out, I find I hate waiting until a long coding task is completed before knowing what model was chosen. I'd prefer to see up front what was chosen so I know the context of the response and reasoning as it's happening, and also so I have a chance to stop it and manually choose a model.

It sometimes uses a tool call instead, too. When Copilot with Claude Sonnet 4.6 retries, it also provides no explanation before the retry, and it tries running the command outside of the sandbox immediately, requesting an approval from the user.

title: GitHub Copilot sees "compilerError" for diagnostics instead of the "C/C++ IntelliSense" diagnostic source
document:
GitHub Copilot sees "compilerError" for diagnostics instead of the "C/C++ IntelliSense" diagnostic source

Our C/C++ extension has a bunch of known bugs with invalid IntelliSense errors due a different compiler being used, but Copilot is being temporarily mislead to believe it's a "compilerError", due to this Copilot diagnostic tagging code below and the lack of it being sent the diagnostic "source" (our current "source" is "C/C++" but I attempted to fix it by changing it to "C/C++ IntelliSense, but Copilot doesn't see that still). Copilot may be able to determine the source from the error text itself given knowledge of what errors the compilers produce, but it's theoretically possible for some error text to be identical without the "source" to differentiate and seems like it could potentially waste tokens.

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/c2f5ceaea30590a330319fb3a769fb953948fd30/extensions/copilot/src/extension/prompts/node/inline/diagnosticsContext.tsx#L94

A potential fix might just be to add the vscode.Diagnostic source to the context.

...and maybe the "compilerError" text should also be renamed to something less specific and not potentially incorrect.

Can someone **officially** provide justification as to why GHCP is grossly lagging behind VS Code, particularly when one IDE is free, and the other is thousands of dollars a year? 

I'm not sure if Microsoft is intentionally trying to rage bait people, but this about did it for me today:

Seriously? 

These are so grossly off that it's nearly impossible to use VS Enterprise, which I prefer for some of the other capabilities, but MSFT has made this an untenable mess where I spend my day in two IDEs on the same body of work. That is not acceptable.

title: Agents view: session resume fails with "Error during execution" when no git repo
document:
Agents view: session resume fails with "Error during execution" when no git repo

## Bug Description

When resuming a CLI-created Claude Code session from the Agents view, the session fails with:

The underlying SDK error is:

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a Claude Code session via CLI in a directory without a git repository (e.g., `C:\Users\username`)
2. Open VS Code Insiders (no workspace open)
3. Open the Agents view — the CLI session appears in the session list
4. Click on the session and send a message (e.g., "hi")
5. **Result**: "Error: Error during execution" is displayed

## Root Cause

When the session has no associated git repository, `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` creates a fallback workspace folder with URI `unknown:///` ([line 1266](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/sessions/contrib/providers/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts#L1266)). `WorkspaceFolderManagementContribution` then registers this as a real workspace folder.

In the Copilot extension, `getFolderInfoForSession()` sees one workspace folder and uses its `fsPath` as the Claude SDK `cwd`. On Windows, `unknown:///` resolves to `fsPath = "\"`. The SDK uses this invalid `cwd` to locate the session file on disk, looking in `~/.claude/projects/\-/` instead of the correct project directory.

## Environment

- VS Code Insiders 1.122.0
- Windows 11
- Claude Code CLI sessions created via Agent Maestro proxy

## Fix

Filter `getWorkspaceFolders()` to only include `file://` scheme URIs in `getFolderInfoForSession()`, so virtual/unknown workspace folders are excluded. See PR.

document:
Workspace search "files to include" always filters to "Only search in open editors" regardless of selection

- VS Code Version: 1.122.1 (Universal)
- OS Version: 1.122.1 (Universal)

1. Open a workspace with multiple folders
2. Use Workspace Search to look for a term that is present in files in one subfolder
3. Use the "files to include" to filter to the sub-folder using glob pattern "subfolder/**"
4. Toggle between "Only search in open editors". Observe how the setting is active regardless of selection.

When using workspace search "files to include", it always filters to "Only search in open editors" regardless of if the setting is selected or not.

title: Agents Window chat input history is shared across sessions
document:
Agents Window chat input history is shared across sessions

When using the Agents Window with multiple sessions open, chat input history is shared across sessions.

## Repro

1. Open the Agents Window.
2. Open or create two different sessions.
3. Send a few prompts in the first session.
4. Switch to the second session and send different prompts there.
5. Press Up Arrow in the chat input to navigate previous prompts.

## Actual

The input history can include prompts from other sessions. For example, pressing Up Arrow in session B can show prompts that were sent in session A.

## Expected

Chat input history should be scoped to the current session. Pressing Up/Down should only navigate prompts submitted in that same session.

## Demo

I have a video below showing the shared-history behavior. (The sessions were in two different folders)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/49f2c394-5709-4088-8e0f-ef581c62254c

title: Rubber Duck setting needs to be an application scoped setting (with current implementation)
document:
Rubber Duck setting needs to be an application scoped setting (with current implementation)

Testing https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/319092

`chat.agentHost.rubberDuck.enabled` is currently not implemented as a application scoped setting. The agent host will have the rubber duck feature enabled for all windows or none as there is only one agent host and not one per window so the setting can not be scoped by profile but has to be per application. If we think about remote agent host then even application scoped will not work.

I wonder why the rubber duck enablement needs to be set when the agent host starts and can not be sent with the request. It seems to me that this approach of specifying all settings when the agent host starts will cause a ton of issues long term. It will not allow for profile based settings and when it comes to remote agent host will complicate things even more. 

cc @roblourens @connor4312

## Steps to Reproduce:

2. Run **Developer: Reload Window** in VS Code
3. Open Copilot Chat and check the agent list

5. **Actual:** Agent does not appear
6. Move the same `test.agent.md` file to `~/.config/Code/User/prompts/` instead
7. Reload window again
8. Agent now appears and loads correctly

## Issue:
Agent discovery scans `User/prompts/` instead of `User/agents/`. Custom agents must be placed in the `prompts` directory to be discovered, but the documented location is `agents/`. This is a path misconfiguration in the agent discovery logic.

title: Please provide a way to override the system prompt, so we can reduce credit waste
document:
Please provide a way to override the system prompt, so we can reduce credit waste

Now that we are paying for tokens, the system prompt is getting in the way. If I run a simple prompt "Respond with a single dot", my billing shows 4.48 credits jump. If I understand the billing correctly, this works out to about 10-15K tokens, which is in this case is almost entirely the system prompt and accompanying data.

What's worse, is that I often need to add more of my tokens to override unacceptable things in the system prompt, like this phrase - "_By default, implement changes rather than only suggesting them._", which I constantly need to override in my prompts or undo the changes, which also wastes tokens. There are other examples of where the system prompt suggests what it shouldn't.

Add a disclaimer that changing the system prompt will "void warranty", and perhaps a simple way to revert to the standard system prompt, which could be required for submitting bug reports, perhaps, but please allow users to edit the system prompt, so we can phrase it more concisely and make it work better for our needs.

title: Tool virtualization should fully support large MCP servers (100s of tools) via semantic lazy-loading
document:
Tool virtualization should fully support large MCP servers (100s of tools) via semantic lazy-loading

The current tool virtualization system (introduced to fix #290356 / #294055) groups deferred tools behind `activate_*` stubs or `tool_search`, but in practice only surfaces a small subset (~14 out of 154 tools) from a single MCP server. The `tool_search` semantic lookup repeatedly returns the same few tools regardless of query variation.

**What should work**: An MCP server with hundreds of tools should function seamlessly. The model already has `tool_search` — it just needs the embeddings index to cover ALL registered tools, not a capped subset. The architecture is correct (lazy discovery → load on demand → call), but the implementation appears to index only ~14 tools from a server that reports 154 via `tools/list`.

**Suggestion**:
1. Index ALL tools from `tools/list` into the virtualization embeddings — don't cap pre-indexing
2. Let `tool_search` return more results (currently seems capped at 5)
3. Allow agents to discover tools via skills (`.instructions.md` / `SKILL.md`) — if a skill references a tool by name, the agent should be able to load and call it via `tool_search` even if it wasn't surfaced in the initial deferred list. This would make hiding tools from the default tool list much less problematic, since domain-specific skills can guide the agent to the right tools on demand.

**Environment**: VS Code 1.123, Copilot Chat latest, MCP server via Streamable HTTP transport, 154 tools registered

title: BidirectionalMap.set() and delete() cannot handle undefined as a stored value
document:
BidirectionalMap.set() and delete() cannot handle undefined as a stored value

- VS Code Version: Code - OSS (source repository, all versions affected)
- OS Version: Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS (Noble Numbat), x86_64, Linux 5.15.120
- Node.js: v24.15.0

## Problem

In `src/vs/base/common/map.ts`, `BidirectionalMap.set()` and `delete()` use
`get() !== undefined` / `get() === undefined` as a sentinel to detect whether
a key exists in the internal `Map`. Since `Map.get()` returns `undefined` both
for missing keys and for keys whose stored value is `undefined`, this creates
two bugs:

### Bug 1 — set() leaks stale reverse mapping

The old `undefined → 'one'` entry in the reverse map is never cleaned up,
causing memory leaks and incorrect `getKey()` results.

### Bug 2 — delete() cannot remove entries whose value is undefined

`delete()` compares `value === undefined` to detect key absence, so entries
with `undefined` as a legitimate stored value can never be deleted.

### Bug 3 — getOrSet() overwrites undefined values

Same sentinel problem in the helper function at the top of the same file.

## Steps to Reproduce

Run the following in any TypeScript environment with the VS Code source
`src/vs/base/common/map.ts` module loaded:

## Expected Behavior

- `getKey(undefined)` should return `undefined` after overwriting an entry
 whose previous value was `undefined`.
- `delete()` should succeed (return `true`) when the entry's stored value
 happens to be `undefined`.
- `getOrSet()` should not overwrite an existing key whose stored value is
 `undefined`.

## Root Cause

The `undefined` sentinel cannot distinguish "key not found" from "key exists
with value == undefined" in JavaScript `Map`. The fix: use `Map.has()`.

## Fix

Replaced `get() === undefined` / `get() !== undefined` checks with `Map.has()`
calls at three locations:

1. `BidirectionalMap.set()` — both oldValue and oldKey checks
2. `BidirectionalMap.delete()` — key existence check
3. `getOrSet()` — key existence check

Added 5 new test cases for undefined-value edge cases in
`src/vs/base/test/common/map.test.ts`.

No API change. No performance regression (`Map.has()` is O(1)).

title: Support SQL Keyword Formatting Inside C# String Literals
document:
Support SQL Keyword Formatting Inside C# String Literals

When writing SQL queries inside C# string literals (especially verbatim strings and raw string literals), it would be helpful if Visual Studio Code and C# tooling could recognize SQL content and provide:

* SQL syntax highlighting
* SQL IntelliSense
* Automatic SQL formatting
* SQL keyword capitalization (SELECT, FROM, WHERE, JOIN, etc.)
* Validation of SQL syntax inside strings

Example:

var query = """
select *
from employee
where id = 1
""";

Expected behavior:

The editor recognizes the content as SQL and optionally formats it as:

SELECT *
FROM employee
WHERE id = 1;

This would significantly improve readability and productivity for developers who frequently embed SQL queries in C# applications.

title: Feature Request: Presaved MCP Toolsets / Named Presets for Context-Aware Tool Activation
document:
Feature Request: Presaved MCP Toolsets / Named Presets for Context-Aware Tool Activation

## Problem

When using multiple MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers, developers often need to switch between different subsets of tools depending on the task at hand. Currently, VS Code only supports toggling entire MCP servers on/off via the `disabled` flag or managing them through workspace settings and profiles.

There is no way to define **named, reusable groups of tools** (e.g., `toolset: "analytics"`, `toolset: "frontend-review"`) and quickly activate or deactivate them without:
- Manually editing `settings.json` to toggle `disabled` on multiple servers.
- Switching entirely to a different VS Code profile, which changes more than just MCP tools.
- Creating separate workspace configurations for every tool combination.

## Proposed Solution

Introduce a **"MCP Toolsets"** (or "Presets") concept that allows users to define named sets of enabled MCP servers/tools. A toolset could be activated via:
1. Command Palette (e.g., `MCP: Activate Toolset`)
2. A quick-pick dropdown in the MCP panel or status bar.
3. API for extensions to switch toolsets programmatically.

### Example Configuration

### Activation Flow

1. User runs `MCP: Activate Toolset` from the Command Palette.
2. VS Code presents the configured toolsets (`backend-development`, `frontend-testing`, etc.).
3. On selection, VS Code updates the active MCP context: servers listed in the toolset are enabled, and those not listed are disabled (or excluded from the current context).
4. Copilot/LM immediately sees only the tools from the active toolset.

## Alternative / Extended Ideas

- **Per-tool granularity**: Beyond server-level grouping, allow toolsets to specify individual tools within a server (e.g., only allow `read_file` from a filesystem server, disable `write_file`).
- **Workspace overrides**: Allow `.vscode/settings.json` to define workspace-specific toolsets while inheriting server definitions from user settings.
- **Dynamic toolsets via extensions**: Let extensions contribute toolsets programmatically (e.g., a project-scoped extension detects it's in a Python repo and auto-suggests the "python-dev" toolset).

## Use Cases

- **Context switching**: A developer working on a backend API in the morning and a frontend bug in the afternoon can switch toolsets instead of manually toggling servers.
- **Reducing token/context bloat**: Limiting active tools to only what's needed keeps the LM context focused and can improve response quality.
- **Team onboarding**: Teams can share `.vscode/settings.json` with pre-made toolsets so new members don't need to know which servers to enable for which tasks.
- **Safe modes**: A "read-only" toolset that only exposes safe tools for review/auditing, versus a "full-access" toolset for active development.

## Current Workarounds and Their Limitations

| Workaround | Limitation |
|---|---|
| Toggle `disabled` per server in `settings.json` | Tedious, error-prone, requires editing JSON manually. |
| VS Code Profiles | Overkill — changes extensions, themes, keybindings, etc., not just MCP tools. |
| Workspace-specific `.vscode/settings.json` | Requires duplicating server configs across workspaces; no way to quickly switch within the same workspace. |

## Related

- MCP specification discussions around tool filtering and server discovery.
- Existing need for MCP server management UI improvements.

---

I'd be happy to help refine this proposal or provide more specific UX mockups if helpful. Thank you for considering this!

document:
Copilot Premium Request Usage Appears Excessive Despite All Models Showing 1x Multiplier

Hi Copilot Team,

I would like to report a potential issue regarding Premium Request consumption.

Issue Description

All available models in Copilot currently display a 1x multiplier in the model selector.
However, after using Copilot for only a single morning session (a few hours of normal coding and chat usage), my Premium Requests usage already reached 37.5% of the monthly allowance.
This consumption rate seems disproportionately high compared to the displayed 1x cost multiplier.

Environment

Copilot Agent Mode
Workspace Index: Basic Index
Models tested include Claude, Gemini, and GPT series, all showing 1x consumption.

Expected Behavior

If a model is labeled as 1x, the premium request consumption should be consistent with that multiplier and usage should be reasonably predictable.

Actual Behavior

Premium Request allowance decreased to approximately 37.5% used after only a short period of usage.
It is unclear whether:
Agent Mode is consuming additional hidden requests,
Background indexing/tool usage is counted separately,
Or the displayed 1x multiplier does not accurately reflect actual consumption.

Screenshot Attached
The attached screenshot shows all available models marked as 1x while Premium Request usage has already reached 37.5%.

Could you please clarify how Premium Requests are being calculated in Agent Mode and investigate whether the usage tracking is functioning correctly?

Thank you.

title: Allow extensions to contribute and read resolvable variables from the workbench, Take #42
document:
Allow extensions to contribute and read resolvable variables from the workbench, Take #42

This feature has been requested by the community over and over again for the past at least 7+ years. We are going to keep asking until we get somewhere, because it seems to keep getting ignored by the team.

Allow a mechanism for extensions to contribute and read resolvable variables to the workbench. This would imply extension X can contribute variables to the workbench that can be used anywhere normal variables can be used such as within tasks.json, settings.json, etc.. Additionally, the mechanism would also allow extensions to resolve variables into values. It goes both ways.

I would be willing to contribute the solution to this issue myself as I have downloaded the vscode repository and have been building a proof of concept to know if this is feasible. However, I am a C, C# developer by trade and have next to no experience in the vscode architecture nor typescript. Therefore, it would be requested that someone in the know actually implement the proper interfaces.

Here are screenshots from my proof of concept

I don't know what will be accepted for the 'mechanism' by which to do this. However this is one such concrete proposal.

1. Add a new variable form to the existing forms such as command, input etc. The form would be ${var:extension.variable}.
2. Extensions can contribute / register a single callback to the workbench that gets executed when the variable form is encountered. The resolver holds a map of extension ids to callbacks
3. The resolver logic traps on the 'var' in the switch statement above and parses the extension id. The resolver looks in the extension map. If the extension exists within the map, it executes the callback
4. Within the extension callback, the extension uses its own logic for tracking contributed variables and returns a string back to the resolver of the variable value. The variable value itself can contain embedded resolvable variables.
5. Extensions can also call a now publicized method on the resolver to resolve a variable and get the value of the variable

The other option would be for the resolver to hold a map that extensions can contribute to directly. However, I think this may cause conflict between extensions if multiple extensions try to contribute or use the same name of a variable.

In the above example, I have a variable in a task called 'thebaz'. To use it within tasks.json the form is ${var:myextension.thebaz}. The variable contains an embedded variable called thefoob. The resolver resolves this to ${var:myextension.thefoob}. The resolver then resolves ${var:myextension.thefoob} to 'FOOBED!' The proof of concept proves this is very doable as the plumbing for the recursive resolution is already in place.

Current trigger for this request is : #46471

title: [BYOK] VS Code Copilot Chat: "Sorry, your request failed" – Server error, Stream terminated – Client Request Id 3afc9c34-3437-4d34-a824-84a018149633
document:
[BYOK] VS Code Copilot Chat: "Sorry, your request failed" – Server error, Stream terminated – Client Request Id 3afc9c34-3437-4d34-a824-84a018149633

## Summary

When using GitHub Copilot Chat in VS Code with **Bring Your Own Key (BYOK)**, I consistently get a server error and the request fails, even after retries.

**Error message:**

This happens repeatedly and prevents me from using Copilot Chat with my BYOK model.

---

## Environment

- **OS:** macOS (version: [e.g. macOS 15.5])
- **VS Code version:** [e.g. 1.106.0] (check via `Code > About Visual Studio Code`)
- **GitHub Copilot extension version:** [e.g. 1.260.0] (check in Extensions panel)
- **Copilot Chat extension version:** [if separate, e.g. 0.26.0]
- **Copilot mode:** BYOK (Bring Your Own Key)
- **Model / provider:** [e.g. GPT-4o via OpenAI, or your exact model name and provider]
- **VS Code installation:** [e.g. official .pkg from code.visualstudio.com, or from Homebrew]

---

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open VS Code on macOS.
2. Ensure I’m signed in with my GitHub account that has Copilot access.
3. Configure Copilot Chat to use **BYOK** with my model: [briefly describe, e.g. “OpenAI API key in settings” or “provider URL and key”].
4. Open the **Copilot Chat** panel.
5. Type a simple prompt, e.g. “Explain this code” or “Generate a function to …”.
6. Send the request.

**Expected behavior:** 
Copilot returns a response.

**Actual behavior:** 
Copilot returns the error above and the stream terminates.

---

## Frequency

- **How often:** [e.g. 100% of the time / every request / intermittently]
- **Since when:** [e.g. started on 2026-05-28, or “since I started using BYOK”]

---

## What I’ve Tried

- Retrying the request multiple times in Copilot Chat [🔄 retry button].
- Starting a **new chat** in Copilot Chat.
- Switching to a different model in the Copilot Chat model selector (if available).
- Reloading VS Code: `Developer: Reload Window`.
- Checking network connection (stable, no proxy / or describe if you use a proxy).
- Running: **GitHub Copilot: Collect Diagnostics** and saving the file.
- Setting log level to **Trace** for **GitHub Copilot** and **GitHub Copilot Chat**, then checking the Output panel.

Nothing has resolved the issue.

---

## Diagnostics & Logs

I have collected:

- **Diagnostics file:** `github-copilot-diagnostics-*.json` (attached)
- **Copilot Chat logs** from the Output panel (`GitHub Copilot Chat` channel), including the full stack trace with the Client Request Id.
- **Client Request Id:** `3afc9c34-3437-4d34-a824-84a018149633`

I can paste redacted logs here or attach them if GitHub allows file uploads.

---

## Additional Context

- I am using **BYOK** to connect to my own model provider.
- The same API key/model works fine when tested directly (e.g., via curl / Postscript / other tool): [yes/no – if yes, briefly describe].
- This appears to be a **server-side stream termination** error in the Copilot extension, not a local code issue.

---

## Request

Could you please:

1. Investigate whether this is a known issue with **Copilot Chat + BYOK** on macOS.
2. Check logs for Client Request Id `3afc9c34-3437-4d34-a824-84a018149633` on the server side.
3. Provide guidance on:
 - Any required configuration for BYOK in VS Code.
 - Whether this is due to a service outage or a bug in the Copilot extension.

title: Remote Tunnel: status stuck on Connecting after successful startup
document:
Remote Tunnel: status stuck on Connecting after successful startup

## Summary

After the Remote Tunnel starts successfully, the status in VS Code remains stuck on "Connecting" indefinitely. The tunnel itself appears to be running and accessible, but the UI never transitions to a ready/connected state.

## Info
- Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes
- VS Code Version: 1.122.1
- OS Version: Windows 11 25H2
- VS Code Tunnel Version: 1.5.3

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start VS Code Remote Tunnel.
2. Wait until the tunnel logs show it has started successfully.
3. Observe the tunnel status in VS Code.

## Expected Behavior

The tunnel status should change from "Connecting" to a ready/connected state after the tunnel has successfully started.

## Actual Behavior

The tunnel starts successfully, but the status remains stuck on "Connecting" forever.

## Additional Notes

- The tunnel URL is generated successfully.
- The tunnel process appears to start normally.
- The issue seems to be that the UI/status indicator does not reflect the successful tunnel startup.

title: terminal.integrated.tabs.title template ignored when process sends OSC title sequences
document:
terminal.integrated.tabs.title template ignored when process sends OSC title sequences

- VS Code Version: latest (reproduced in Cursor, which is VS Code-based)
- OS Version: macOS 24.6.0

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Set `"terminal.integrated.tabs.title": "${cwdFolder}"` in settings.json
2. Open the integrated terminal
3. Run a process that sends OSC escape sequences to set the terminal title (e.g. Claude Code CLI)
4. Observe the tab title

## Expected behavior

The tab should show the folder name (`${cwdFolder}`), as configured. Non-`${sequence}` templates should take precedence over OSC sequences sent by processes.

## Actual behavior

The tab title is overwritten by the process's OSC sequence and stays that way for the entire session. When multiple sessions are open across different projects, all tabs are indistinguishable.

## Notes

- Using `${sequence}` explicitly works as expected (shows the process-set title)
- The bug only affects cases where the user has chosen a template other than `${sequence}` with the expectation that it overrides process titles

Related: anthropics/claude-code#64448

title: Chat: Custom session titles are lost after extension update / index rebuild
document:
Chat: Custom session titles are lost after extension update / index rebuild

## Bug Description

When you rename a chat session (right-click → Rename), the custom title is saved to the SQLite index (`chat.ChatSessionStore.index` in `state.vscdb`) but is **not written back to the corresponding `.jsonl` session file** (`customTitle` field in the first line).

When the Copilot Chat extension updates or rebuilds its index, it reads session metadata from the `.jsonl` files. Since `customTitle` is missing there, all renamed sessions revert to the default title ("New Chat" / "新建聊天").

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open the Chat panel and start a session.
2. Right-click the session → Rename it to something custom (e.g. "My Feature Work").
3. Update the Copilot Chat extension or reload VS Code enough times to trigger an index rebuild.
4. Observe that the session title reverts to "New Chat".

## Expected Behavior

The `customTitle` field should be written to both the SQLite index **and** the `.jsonl` session file atomically when a rename occurs. This ensures the title survives any index rebuild.

## Actual Behavior

Only the SQLite index is updated. The `.jsonl` file's `customTitle` field remains empty/absent. After an index rebuild the title is lost.

## Root Cause (investigated locally)

- Session files: `~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/workspaceStorage//chatSessions/.jsonl`
- Index: `~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/workspaceStorage//state.vscdb` → `ItemTable` key `chat.ChatSessionStore.index`
- After renaming, the `.jsonl` first-line JSON has no `customTitle` while the database entry does. Verified across 8 sessions.

## Workaround

Manually patch the `.jsonl` files to add the missing `customTitle` using a script that reads the database and writes back to the files.

## Environment

- OS: macOS
- VS Code version: latest
- Extension: GitHub Copilot Chat

title: BYOK Azure provider: Support Anthropic-format deployments on Azure AI Services
document:
BYOK Azure provider: Support Anthropic-format deployments on Azure AI Services

- Copilot Chat Extension Version:
- VS Code Version: 1.122.1
- OS Version: Windows 11 Business
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): any
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): BYOK / Azure
- Logs: n/a
## Problem

Claude models deployed via Azure AI Foundry (GlobalStandard) only expose the Anthropic Messages API (`/anthropic/v1/messages`). The BYOK Azure provider cannot use them because:

1. `resolveAzureUrl` in [azureProvider.ts](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/vscode-node/azureProvider.ts) doesn't recognize `services.ai.azure.com` — throws "Unrecognized Azure deployment URL". This one is easily fixed, but...
2. Even with an explicit URL, the Azure provider only speaks OpenAI chat completions format — there's no translation to Anthropic Messages API. This requires a message adapter or use of the existing Anthropic Provider (adapted to Azure).

## Expected Behavior

`vendor: "azure"` should support Anthropic-format deployments. When the configured URL contains `/anthropic/`, the Azure provider should use the Anthropic Messages protocol instead of OpenAI chat completions.

## Steps to Reproduce:

1. Deploy a claude model, e.g. Opus 4.6, via Azure AI Foundry → GlobalStandard deployment
2. Portal shows Target URI: `https://.services.ai.azure.com/anthropic/v1/messages`
3. Add model in `chatLanguageModels.json` with `vendor: "azure"`
4. Any URL attempted results in either "Unrecognized Azure deployment URL" or 404

## Possible Solution
A bit of AI assisted speculation in a possible solution (untested). The existing `AnthropicLMProvider` ([anthropicProvider.ts](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/vscode-node/anthropicProvider.ts)) already implements the full Anthropic streaming protocol, tool calls, thinking, etc. The Azure provider could reuse that code path:

1. In `resolveAzureUrl`, recognize `services.ai.azure.com` and pass through URLs containing `/anthropic/`
2. In `AzureBYOKModelProvider.provideLanguageModelChatResponse`, detect when the resolved URL is an Anthropic endpoint
3. Instantiate the Anthropic SDK client with `baseURL` set to the Azure URL (e.g. `https://.services.ai.azure.com/anthropic`) and `apiKey` from the Azure config
4. Delegate to the same streaming/tool-call logic already in `AnthropicLMProvider._makeRequest`

title: BYOK requests consume Copilot quota on OpenAI-compatible endpoints
document:
BYOK requests consume Copilot quota on OpenAI-compatible endpoints

## Problem

BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) requests routed through OpenAI-compatible endpoints (Azure, OpenAI, OpenRouter, custom endpoints) still deduct from the user's Copilot monthly quota.

## Root cause

The request flow goes through CAPI (Copilot proxy):

`
CopilotLanguageModelWrapper → OpenAIEndpoint (extends ChatEndpoint) → chatMLFetcher → CAPI
`

Although OpenAIEndpoint sets ownsAuthorization = true (skipping the CAPI Copilot bearer token), the request still goes through the CAPI proxy pipeline. CAPI returns copilot_usage.total_nano_aiu in the response, which chatMLFetcher records via IChatQuotaService.setLastCopilotUsage(), reducing the user's available quota.

## Key code paths

- \extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/node/openAIEndpoint.ts\ — BYOK endpoint with \ownsAuthorization = true\
- \extensions/copilot/src/extension/prompt/node/chatMLFetcher.ts\ lines ~387-432 — CAPI response processing consumes \copilot_usage.total_nano_aiu\
- \extensions/copilot/src/platform/chat/common/chatQuotaService.ts\ — quota tracking service

## Notable exception

Anthropic BYOK does **not** consume quota because \AnthropicLMProvider\ calls the Anthropic SDK directly (SDK → Anthropic API), completely bypassing CAPI.

## Expected behavior

When a user provides their own API key, the request should not consume Copilot quota since no Copilot infrastructure is being billed.

title: Copliot Chat, path rendering configuration options
document:
Copliot Chat, path rendering configuration options

Currently, when my Copliot Chat completes an analysis over a monorepo, it produces detailed report tables that often contain relative paths to the locations where certain patterns I have asked about occur.

The Chat interface helpfully renders these with a folder icon and makes them clickable to jump straight to the file, which is handy when looking at a specific example from a table.

Less helpfully, the hardcoded behaviour is to only retain the very last directory name for the target path, and the agent I am talking to has no way to control or override this behaviour other than to intentionally do a raw ASCII render of the table.

**Why is this a problem?**
My mono-repo has some regular recurring structures in it that often end in identical directory names as the last step of a path. When the AI renders a table and the VSCode Chat interface only shows the last directory, I end up with tables that only say "`api`", which is completely useless to read/scan for information as all the useful context has been stripped to tell me which part of the system the API is part of.

**What I'd like**
Some kind of configuration setting in VSCode to control this stripping behaviour, either a way to tell it to keep N directories above the last, or a way to suppress this behaviour completely.

Note, I like the fact these are links to directories/files that I can click to jump around in the codebase, and I'd like to retain that behaviour, but the stripping down to only the last directory/file name is what is making this feature result in useless output.

title: Test: Copilot Chat offers Ubuntu 24.04 bubblewrap sandbox repair
document:
Test: Copilot Chat offers Ubuntu 24.04 bubblewrap sandbox repair

Refs: microsoft/vscode#319061

- [x] Linux @aeschli

Complexity: 3

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23319256%0A%0A&assignees=dileepyavan)

---

Verify that Copilot Chat detects when `bubblewrap` is installed but unusable on Ubuntu 24.04 because of AppArmor / user namespace restrictions, offers repair actions before running a sandboxed terminal command, re-checks sandbox prerequisites after repair, and does not silently fail or run the command before the sandbox is usable.

### Prerequisites

- Latest VS Code Insiders on Ubuntu 24.04
- Sign in to GitHub Copilot
- Use a trusted workspace folder
- Use Copilot Chat in agent mode
- Use a disposable VM or test machine because this test may install AppArmor packages and modify system sandbox policy
- Ensure Linux sandboxing is enabled:
 - `chat.agent.sandbox.enabled` = `on`
- Ensure `bubblewrap` and `socat` are installed, but `bubblewrap` cannot create the sandbox namespace. For example, the following command should fail with a namespace / permission error such as `bwrap: setting up uid map: Permission denied`:
 

### Scenario 1: unusable bubblewrap offers repair and does not run the command first

1. Open a Copilot Chat agent mode session in the workspace.
2. Prompt:
 > Run `echo sandbox-ok` in the terminal.
3. When prompted, first choose **Cancel**.

**Expected:**
- A confirmation titled similar to **Repair Bubblewrap Sandbox** appears before the command is executed.
- The confirmation explains that bubblewrap is installed but cannot create the required sandbox namespace.
- After choosing **Cancel**, `echo sandbox-ok` is not run.
- Copilot Chat reports that the bubblewrap sandbox repair was cancelled, rather than showing a silent terminal failure.

### Scenario 2: AppArmor repair re-checks prerequisites and then runs sandboxed

1. Re-issue the same prompt:
 > Run `echo sandbox-ok` in the terminal.
2. Choose **Apply AppArmor Fix**.
3. If prompted for a sudo password, use the focused/revealed terminal to enter it.

**Expected:**
- VS Code runs the AppArmor remediation command in a terminal and waits for it to complete.
- After the remediation succeeds, VS Code re-checks sandbox prerequisites before running the original command.
- The original `echo sandbox-ok` command then runs and completes successfully inside the sandbox.
- Copilot Chat shows `sandbox-ok` in the result.

### Scenario 3: repaired sandbox works on the next command

1. In the same workspace, prompt Copilot Chat agent mode again:
 > Run `echo sandbox-still-ok` in the terminal.

**Expected:**
- No bubblewrap repair prompt is shown.
- The command runs in the sandbox and returns `sandbox-still-ok`.

title: Custom Endpoint vision attachments stop working when using BYOK without GitHub sign-in
document:
Custom Endpoint vision attachments stop working when using BYOK without GitHub sign-in

Summary

Image attachments are not passed to a custom endpoint model when using the new BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) functionality without a GitHub sign-in.

The exact same model and endpoint work correctly when signed into GitHub.

Configuration

chatLanguageModels.json

[
 {
 "name": "Custom Endpoint",
 "vendor": "customendpoint",
 "apiType": "chat-completions",
 "models": [
 {
 "id": "miia",
 "name": "miia2",
 "url": "https://",
 "toolCalling": true,
 "vision": true,
 "maxInputTokens": 180000,
 "maxOutputTokens": 80000
 }
 ]
 }
]

1. Configure a custom endpoint model with vision=true.
2. Sign into GitHub.
3. Paste an image into Copilot Chat.
4. Ask the model to describe the image.
5. Observe that vision works correctly.
6. Sign out of GitHub.
7. Paste the same image into Copilot Chat.
8. Ask the same question.

Expected Result

The image should be sent to the custom endpoint regardless of GitHub authentication state.

Actual Result

The image attachment appears in the chat UI ("Pasted Image"), but the model behaves as if no image was provided.

Additional Notes

- Text chat works correctly.
- Tool calling works correctly.
- The same endpoint and model work correctly when signed into GitHub.
- The issue only occurs when using BYOK without GitHub sign-in.

title: Test: Copilot Chat retries network-blocked commands inside the sandbox
document:
Test: Copilot Chat retries network-blocked commands inside the sandbox

Refs: microsoft/vscode#319181

- [x] macOS @jruales
- [x] Linux @rzhao271 

Complexity: 3

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23319254%0A%0A&assignees=dileepyavan)

---

Verify that Copilot Chat terminal commands that need network access first retry inside the sandbox with unrestricted network access after user confirmation, instead of immediately falling back to unsandboxed execution.

### Prerequisites

- Latest VS Code Insiders
- Sign in to GitHub Copilot
- Open a trusted workspace folder
- Use Copilot Chat in agent mode
- Use macOS or Linux (Windows sandboxing currently enables sandboxing with network access, so this network-blocking scenario is not applicable there)
- Ensure the sandbox is enabled but network is **not** globally allowed:
 - `chat.agent.sandbox.enabled` = `on`
 - `chat.agent.sandbox.retryWithAllowNetworkRequests` = `true` (default)
 - `chat.agent.sandbox.allowUnsandboxedCommands` = `true` (default)
 - Do **not** add the test domain below to the sandbox network allowlist
- Open a **Copilot Chat** agent mode session in the workspace and keep it open for the whole test

### Scenario 1: blocked network command requests allow-network sandbox confirmation

1. In the Copilot Chat agent mode session, prompt:
 > Run `git fetch origin`.

**Expected:**
- VS Code either detects the blocked domain before running, or the first sandboxed attempt is blocked by network restrictions.
- Copilot Chat retries by requesting to run the command sandboxed with unrestricted network access (`requestAllowNetwork`) and provides a reason.
- The confirmation/details mention unrestricted network access in the sandbox.
- The command remains sandboxed; the confirmation is **not** for unsandboxed execution.
- After approving, the command succeeds and shows an HTTP status line (for example `HTTP/2 200` or `HTTP/1.1 200 OK`).

### Scenario 2: disabling allow-network retry uses the old unsandboxed fallback

1. Set `chat.agent.sandbox.retryWithAllowNetworkRequests` = `false`.
2. Start a new Copilot Chat agent mode session in the same workspace.
3. Re-issue the same prompt as in Scenario 1.

**Expected:**
- Copilot Chat does **not** request `requestAllowNetwork` / unrestricted network access inside the sandbox.
- If the command cannot run because of sandbox network restrictions, the fallback confirmation is for unsandboxed execution.
- The command does **not** silently run outside the sandbox without confirmation.

title: Mermaid diagram re-renders on every token during Copilot Chat streaming response
document:
Mermaid diagram re-renders on every token during Copilot Chat streaming response

### Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No (requires GitHub Copilot Chat)

### VS Code Version

1.122.1 (Universal, arm64) — commit `8761a5560cfd65fdd19ce7e2bd18dab5c0a4d84e`

### OS Version

macOS 26.5 (build 25F71), Apple Silicon

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Copilot Chat (Ask or Agent mode).
2. Send a prompt that asks the model to produce a response containing a mermaid diagram followed by additional text, e.g.:
 > Explain MVC and include a mermaid diagram of the request flow, then describe each component in detail.
3. Observe the chat panel while the response is streaming.

### Expected Behavior

Once a mermaid ` ` was received.

The flicker stops only after the stream ends. Collapsing and re-expanding the message afterwards produces a single clean render that is stable.

### Suspected Root Cause

The chat markdown renderer appears to re-parse and re-render the **entire** assistant message on each streaming chunk, instead of diffing closed code-block nodes. Because mermaid has no internal content-hash cache, every render call produces a new SVG with a new id and replaces the DOM node, leading to the perceived flicker.

Possible fixes:

- Skip re-rendering a fenced code block whose source has not changed since the last render (content-hash cache at the markdown renderer / chat view level), **or**
- Add a short-circuit inside the mermaid renderer wrapper: `if (lastSource === currentSource) return cachedSvg;`

### Additional Context / Related Issues

This is distinct from the existing mermaid flicker reports, which describe a one-time flash at load time:

- #316964 — Spastic flickering of mermaid diagram
- #316977 — Mermaid block flickers when opening markdown file
- #317083 — Mermaid Preview: graph flashed and disappeared when a fresh preview window opens

The scenario reported here is **specific to Copilot Chat streaming responses** and persists continuously throughout the stream, not just at first render.

cc @mjbvz

title: Agents window: corrupted session file silently loads as empty instead of surfacing error
document:
Agents window: corrupted session file silently loads as empty instead of surfacing error

## Bug Description

When opening an old session in the Agents window, if the Copilot SDK rejects `session.resume` due to a validation error in the session file, the agent host silently falls back to `createSession` with the same ID. This results in the session appearing to load successfully but showing **0 turns** — all conversation history is lost without any user-facing error.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Have an existing agent session with conversation history
2. The session file becomes corrupted (in this case, a compaction metadata field fails validation)
3. Open the session in the Agents window
4. Session appears empty — no error shown to user

## Expected Behavior

The user should be informed that the session could not be restored due to corruption, rather than silently presenting an empty session.

## Actual Behavior

The session loads with 0 turns. The user sees a blank conversation with no indication that data was lost.

## Relevant Logs

## Analysis

The fallback logic is in `src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/copilot/copilotAgent.ts` around line 1626:

The comment says _"The SDK fails to resume sessions that have no messages"_ — but error code `-32603` is also returned for **data corruption**, not just empty sessions. The fallback does not distinguish between these cases.

## Suggested Fix

Two potential approaches:
1. **Agent Host**: Parse the error message to distinguish "no messages" from "corrupted" — surface a notification to the user when data is actually corrupted rather than silently creating an empty session
2. **SDK**: The validation on `batchSize > 0` in compaction metadata should not prevent loading the actual conversation turns — this is metadata, not content

title: Public API to resolve the source resource URI behind a built-in webview preview tab
document:
Public API to resolve the source resource URI behind a built-in webview preview tab

Provide a public, supported way for an extension to obtain the source resource URI backing a built-in webview-preview tab — primarily the classic Markdown preview.

### Current behavior (verified on VS Code 1.122)

- The Markdown preview **editor** (`vscode.openWith` with viewType `vscode.markdown.preview.editor`; editor-title toggle "Reopen as source file") surfaces as a `TabInputCustom`, and `TabInputCustom.uri` exposes the source document URI. This case works today. ✅
- The **classic** Markdown preview (`markdown.showPreview` / `markdown.showPreviewToSide`, Ctrl+Shift+V / Ctrl+K V) surfaces as a `TabInputWebview` with `viewType` = `"mainThreadWebview-markdown.preview"`. `TabInputWebview` exposes only `viewType` (a string) and **no `uri`**. There is no public API to map this webview tab back to the `.md` source it renders. ❌
- For a full-tab `markdown.showPreview`, the source document is often **not** in `window.visibleTextEditors` (it can be `[]`), so the "use the single visible editor" heuristic fails. `showPreviewToSide` keeps the source visible, but that is only one sub-case.

`vscode.window.activeTextEditor` is `undefined` for webview-backed tabs, so the net effect is that there is no supported path from the active classic-preview tab to its source resource URI.

### Use case

Extensions that follow the active document — custom outline trees, structural navigators, table-of-contents / breadcrumb panels — go blank when the user switches to a Markdown preview, even though the preview clearly knows which file it is rendering and that file is open in the workspace.

### Proposed API (any one would resolve this)

1. Add a `uri` / `resource` (or `sourceUri`) field to `TabInputWebview` for built-in previews, mirroring `TabInputCustom.uri`.
2. A general `window.tabGroups` accessor exposing the resource a webview tab is associated with.
3. A Markdown-specific active-resource API (e.g. `onDidChangeActiveMarkdownPreview` + `activeMarkdownPreviewResource`).

### Related issue

#201884 (OPEN) — `TabInputWebview` viewType identification for the Markdown preview; adjacent, but about the `viewType` string, not the source URI.

Suggested labels: `api`, `webview`, `markdown`.

title: Using a non-string for `contributes.commands[*].enablement` silently breaks extension commands
document:
Using a non-string for `contributes.commands[*].enablement` silently breaks extension commands

- VS Code Version: 1.122.1
- OS Version: MacOS Tahoe 26.5

See https://github.com/pypa/hatch-code/issues/194#issue-4556103001 for details

This is pretty severe since it’s silent and affects all extensions loaded after the offending one.

> 1. Install the Hatch extension (`pypa.hatch`) v0.2.0 alongside a handful of other extensions that contribute commands (e.g. rust-analyzer, CodeLLDB, RobotCode).
> 2. Reload / start VS Code.
> 3. Open the Command Palette (Ctrl+Shift+P) and search for a command that is normally provided by one of the other installed extensions (e.g. `rust-analyzer: Run`, `LLDB: …`, `RobotCode: …`). The command is missing — none of that extension's commands are listed anymore.
> 4. Confirm in the log: Command Palette → **Developer: Open Logs Folder** → newest `window*/renderer.log` (or **Developer: Toggle Developer Tools** → Console). You will see a `TypeError: this._input.charCodeAt is not a function` followed by a `Menu item references a command \`…\` which is not defined in the 'commands' section.` line for each affected menu item (see the log excerpt below).
